name: Ancient Vanara
racial_traits:
• Ability Score Modifiers: Ancient vanara are agile and insightful, but their simple lifestyle is not suited for academic studies. They gain +2 Dexterity, +2 Wisdom, and –2 Intelligence.

• Type: Ancient vanara are humanoids with the akashic and vanara subtype.

• Size: Ancient vanara are Medium creatures and thus have no bonuses or penalties due to their size.

• Speed: Ancient vanara have a base speed of 30 feet and a climb speed of 20 feet.

• Akashic Immunity: As akashic creatures, ancient vanara are immune to magical sleep effects.

• Ancestor Species: As the progenitors of modern vanara, ancient vanara count as vanara for the purposes of meeting prerequisites and for selecting alternate racial traits and favored class bonuses.

• Dual Nature (Ex): Born from a creature that tempered his powerful emotions with utmost discipline, ancient vanara get a +1 racial bonus to saves against any spell or effect with the emotion, fear or mind-affecting descriptors, and the DC to intimidate them increases by +1. Ancient vanara can invest essence into this ability; for each point of essence invested, both bonuses increase by one.

• Low-Light Vision: An ancient vanara can see twice as far as a human in dim light.

• Prehensile Tail: An ancient vanara has a long, flexible tail that they can use to carry objects. They cannot wield weapons with their tail, but the tail allows them to retrieve a small, stowed object carried on their person as a swift action.

• Racial Veils: Ancient vanara capable of shaping veils add the Aerial Nimbus and Staff of the Hanumapti to their veil list.

• Bonus Essence: The ancient vanara have a natural connection to the unique power of akasha and start play with 1 bonus essence point in their pool.

• Languages: Ancient vanara begin play speaking Common and Vanaran. Vanaras with high Intelligence scores can choose from the following: Aklo, Celestial, Elven, Gnome, Goblin, and Sylvan.

Alternate Racial Traits
description:
Physical Description: Depending on who you ask, Vanara look like simian-like humans or humanoid monkeys. Having a low capacity to store body fat, they prefer the warmer climates of jungle forests. Their bodies are covered in fur, with many different colors possible. Ivory, chestnut, russet, with the rare black and gold. They also grow hair (of the same color of their fur) like humans, and often wear it long and in elaborate hairstyles. Vanaras have long, prehensile tails and dexterous feet that can be used almost as hands. They tend to be slightly shorter and lighter than the average human. Vanara prefer to wear loose robes that let them move unimpeded.

Society: Vanaras live in small tree towns, with a local "king" in every village. They tend to have few, loose laws, and there is rarely crime in their communities. They survive as hunters and gatherers, and also trade among their people and other communities.

Relations: Vanara tend to get along well with all non-violent races. They have good relations with humans, halflings, and especially gnomes. They have an unquenchable hatred for rakshasas, since these fiends always show unrestrained urges that the vanara find despicable.

Alignment and Religion: Vanara tend towards neutrality. Most vanaras are at least partially neutral. They are not overly religious, with their "priests" normally being druids, oracles, radiants or shamans. The few ones that become religious are loyal to their faiths to a fault.

Adventurers: Vanara gravitate to classes that let them benefit from their natural agility. Brawlers, gurus, monks, rangers, rogues and swashbucklers make half of the adventuring vanaras, but all of the classes have had notable representatives among the monkey folk.

Male Names: Angada, Kesari, Macchanu, Taar

Female Names: Anjana, Mainda, Ruma, Tara

name: Antholaos
racial_traits:
Standard Racial Traits

• Ability Score Modifiers: Antholaoi are very physical folk. At first level, any member of this race can choose to gain a +2 bonus to their Constitution score. Alternatively, females as the bigger and stronger of the sexes, can choose to gain a +2 to their Strength instead. Males, who are shorter and slender, can opt for a +2 to their Dexterity.

• Size: The flowerfolk are medium creatures and have no penalties or bonuses due to their size.

• Type: Antholaoi have the plant type but lack the immunities to mind-affecting, paralysis, poison, polymorph, sleep and stunning effects that type usually has. They also have the akashic subtype, but don't have low-light vision and are not immune to sleep effects.

• Normal Speed: Antholaoi have a speed of 30 ft.

• Languages: Antholaoi begin play speaking Sylvan and either Elven or Gnomish. Flowerfolk with high Intelligence scores can choose any non-secret language they want. They also can establish a rudimentary communication via scent with other Antholaoi, sharing one-word ideas like “danger”, “friend” or “escape”. Creatures with the Scent special ability can learn to understand this Scent language as if it was a standard language, and can even communicate in it if they have an Aroma veil shaped. This is not a language-dependent effect.

Defense Racial Traits

• Dense (Ex): Antholaoi have very dense, wood-like flesh. This grants them Damage Reduction 1/slashing.

• Plant-like Resistance: While not immune, antholaoi plant heritage makes them resistant to certain effects, gaining a +1 racial bonus to mind-affecting, paralysis, poison, polymorph, sleep and stunning effects.

Magical Racial Traits

• Aromatic (Su): Each antholaos is born with a specific, magical scent. When created, each antholaos secretes an akashic aroma, represented by a racial veil with the aroma descriptor. This veil is treated as if invested with a point of essence. Each aroma veil is normally associated with a specific type of flower, mentioned in the veils' entries, but other appropriate ones are possible. Others can be chosen with the GM's permission. This veil does not count against their normal limits on veils shaped, or their veil slots. They use their Constitution as their veilweaving modifier for this veil (or their normal veilweaving modifier, whichever is higher), and their character level as their veilweaver level. Antholaoi can have another aroma veil shaped, but must decide which one they have active at the start of their turn. When this ability is active, an antholaos' own aroma protects them from other spells and effects based on scent, granting them immunity to such effects.

• Essence (Su): Antholaoi begin play with a point of essence.

Other Racial Traits

• Stasigenesis (Ex): Unlike other humanoids, antholaoi do not suffer standard aging penalties. Starting at 300 years of age, and every 100 years thereafter, their cellular structure begins to lignify (turn to wood). Females become dense and massive, gaining +2 Strength but suffering a –4 penalty to Dexterity. Males, on the other hand, become light and whip-like, gaining +2 Dexterity but suffering a –4 penalty to Strength. Finally, for every 100 years past age 300, they also gain a +1 bonus to Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma. If an antholaos reaches 0 in a physical ability score, they don't die, but become immobile and enter into a stupor. Then, they are taken to the Grove Counsel, where they become part of the collective mind.

Senses Racial Traits

• Scent (Ex): Antholaoi can detect approaching enemies, sniff out hidden foes, and track by sense of smell. They can identify familiar odors just as humans do familiar sights. They can detect creatures within 30 feet by sense of smell. If the creature is upwind, the range increases to 60 feet; if downwind, it drops to 15 feet.

Thematically Fitting Archetypes

• Alchemist: Horticulturist

• Cavalier: Castellan, Courtly Knight, Daring General, Gendarme, Green Knight, Honor Guard

• Druid: Blight Druid, Swarm Monger

• Fighter: Phalanx Soldier, Tactician

• Hunter: Verminous Hunter

• Inquisitor: Green Faith Marshal

• Radiant: Enervant

• Witch: Thorns patron
description:
At the dawn of creation, in the deepest regions of the then-barren realm of the fey, the first seed was planted. This seed fed on the soil, nurtured by the very energies of life, and it grew many flowers, each of a different kind. From those flowers, many other trees and plants started to grow. The strongest seeds, those with the greatest infusion of life energies, gained sentience and could move on their own. After many generations, these protohumanoid plants became the Antholaoi, a species that soon populated the fey realm. Unlike mammals, their females were stronger and taller, which led to the development of a matriarchal society. Their personalities were also primal and warlike, which brought them into conflict with other fey races.

A powerful female saw the need to rein those aggressive instincts of her people, and began forcefully training the strongest of females and conditioning them with military drills. That female came to be known as the Flower Empress, leader of a budding empire that again warred against the other fair folk. A couple of the elder fey became so fed up with the hostilities of the Antholaoi that they trapped them in a maze of thorns, deep within the fey realm. But now and then some of the flowerfolk face the dangers of the thorn maze or escape their realm with magic, and contact the outside world.

Physical Description: Antholaoi resemble elves in their overall body shape, being tall, slender and strong. They have, however, green skin of various tones and completely black eyes. The most distinctive feature of the flower-folk is their head. They have a single, big fragrant flower instead of hair. This flower starts as a bud in childhood, and starts opening during puberty. They also never die of old age, but start losing movement with time and become immobile at the end of their long life cycle. When this happens, they are taken to a special grove and become part of a hivemind that serves as adviser to the Flower Empress.

Society: The flower-folk have a matriarchal hereditary monarchy that values strength, and have a military meritocracy of female flower knights. Their carnivorous nature makes farming unnecessary, since they don't eat vegetables, but civilians that tend giant insects make the bulk of society. They use insects as cattle, steeds and beasts of burden. Since they have no blacksmiths or carpenters, they also make use of exoskeletons and living plants in the building of furniture and arms. They also cultivate tools,weapons, and other items from plants with a special process that makes them as strong as steel. Despite being non-metalic, this process results in items made from a living, supernaturally dense plant matter commonly called Grassteel.

Relations: The flower-folk respect strength and order, but hate wanton destruction and debauchery. This puts them at odds with most of the races of the fey realm. As a whole, they tolerate dwarves, elves and gnomes, but actively dislike goblinoids, humans and orcs. They revere treants and dryads as kindred and allies, who often serve as mediators with the rest of the fey. They act with curiosity toward other intelligent plants like mandragorans and ghorans, wondering why they aren't part of the empire already.

Alignment and Religion: Antholaoi military meritocracy translates into a society with strong lawful tendencies that doesn't tolerate chaotic individuals. They don't have a religion in the strict sense of the word, but venerate nature itself. This veneration of nature is extended to the Grove Counsel in a manner akin to some forms of ancestor worship. Some individuals revere the eldest of the fey as gods.

Adventurers: Seeing an antholaos outside of the Forest of Thorns is rare. Most are errant knights on a mission for the empress. The remainder are individuals who don't fit into society, generally due to a disposition towards neutrality or chaos. The flower-folk are very physical, so classes that use this strength abound among them.

Classes that derive their power from nature, like druids, hunters and rangers are also common. Fire magic is taboo among the flower folk, similar to how necromancers are seen among humanoid races, and any individual that shows power over fire is executed.

Female Names: Akonite, Althaea, Anemone, Hellebore, Kallina, Nerion, Xeranth

Male Names: Crocus, Dianthus, Erythron, Hyacinth, Linon, Narcis, Thalass

name: Atlantean
ability_modifiers: +2 racial bonus to one ability score
racial_traits:
• Ability Scores: Being an offshoot of humanity, Atantean characters gain a +2 racial bonus to one ability score of their choice at creation to represent their varied nature.

• Essence Infused: Atlanteans are humanoids with the akashic and human subtypes.

• Medium: Atlanteans are medium creatures and have no bonuses or penalties due to their size.

• Base Speed: Atlanteans have a base speed of 30 feet.

• Aquatic Adaptation: Due to their extensive use of akashic techniques and orichalcum, Atlanteans quickly adapted to life beneath the waves and have passed this as an instinctual response to their descendants. While completely submerged in liquid, and for 1d6 rounds thereafter, Atlanteans are considered to have the Mermaid's Tail veil shaped and do not count it against their total veils shaped or their veil slots. When shaped in this way, this veil lacks a visual manifestation and gains the undetectable descriptor. If an Atlantean can bind veils to the Feet or Belt chakras, they also gain the benefits of having this veil bound to one of those chakras as long as they could normally bind veils to it. Since this veil does not count against the Atlantean’s veil slots or chakras, they can still shape and bind a different veil into the same slot as it freely.

• Orichalcum In The Blood: All Atlanteans have traces of the akashically reactive metal orichalcum in their blood and start play with 1 bonus essence point in their pool. Additionally, Atlanteans that gain levels in a veilweaving class, or that take an archetype or class template that grants the ability to shape veils, gain the ability to shape the minor veil Penannular of Prestidigitation in addition to their normally shaped veils. This minor veil does not count against their total veils shaped.

• Natural Specialist: Atlanteans have a strong tendency towards pursuing excellence in their specialties and receive Skill Focus as a bonus feat at 1st level.

• Undersea Vision: Atlanteans have darkvision 120 feet when underwater, but otherwise have no darkvision at all.

• Languages: Atlanteans are more insular than humans and begin play speaking Common and Atlantean. Atlanteans with high Intelligence scores can choose from the following: Aboleth, Aquan, Dwarven, Elven, Gnome, Halfling, and Orcish.

Non-Human Atlanteans

While the principal population of Atlantis was human, there would have been a representative number of other races. However, due to the insularity of their culture and isolation following the sinking of Atlantis, interbreeding would have resulted in most racial differences being largely cosmetic. Non-human descended Atlanteans have appearances that would be considered normal for members of their racial heritage, but possess the same statistics as human-descended Atlanteans with the exception of age and effects dependent on age (e.g. aging effects), along with size and effects dependent on size (e.g. the damage die of natural attacks). For example, a "halfling" Atlantean reaches middle age at 50 years and is a Small creature, but is otherwise statistically identical to the base Atlantean race.

Akashic Racial Traits

All akashic creatures have the following racial traits unless noted otherwise:

• Essence Pool equal to racial hit die.

• Immunity to sleep effects.

• Low-light vision.

• Unless otherwise noted, akashic creatures always use Charisma as their primary veilweaving modifier. Akashic creatures with class levels use the primary veilweaving modifier of their class.
description:
Such was the vast power which the god settled in the lost island of Atlantis... — Critias

When the Olympian gods were dividing the lands among themselves, Poseidon was granted the island that would become known as Atlantis. This land was rich in resources including having vast amounts of the very rare metal orichalcum.

Atlanteans are an offshoot of humanity that due to hubris, akashic energies, and a divinely created cataclysm have adapted to existing beneath the waves. While this did slightly humble them, they managed to maintain much of the akashic and technological advancement they attained at the height of their power. Since sinking beneath the waves, Atlanteans have developed a tendency towards isolationism and xenophobia that would beggar most Elven nations.

Physical Description: Atlanteans are largely physically indistinguishable from humans they are descended from. Though as a whole they do tend towards olive complexions. Additionally, due to the properties of the trace amounts of orichalcum in their blood, Atlanteans also have longer lifespans than humans.

Society: When the gods turned their wrath upon Atlantis they did not manage to destroy its civilization. In spite of their fortunes, the Atlanteans held onto most if not all of the advancements they made. Due to this modern Atlantis can be considered a post-scarcity civilization, and mystical and technological marvels are commonplace on the sunken isle and its remaining territories. This fact informs some of their general feeling of superiority over other races.

The overwhelming majority of Atlanteans live in relative comfort and hard labor is nearly unheard of, as such tasks are left up to constructs and various devices. For this reason, Atlantean society puts a strong emphasis on the pursuit of intellectual and artistic labors. Atlantis is ruled over by a council of kings, which is headed by a high-king who is a direct descendant of the first son of Poseidon and Cleto.

Relations: While Atlanteans are generally similar to their human cousins, they have lost their expansionist tendencies and have relatively static populations. However, they do tend to be more arrogant and self absorbed than humans. This is both enhanced and tempered their general attitude of noblesse oblige towards their “lessers”.

Atlanteans almost never allow outsiders into their watery domain and are very secretive about their homeland when asked for details about it.

Alignment and Religion: Atlanteans tend toward lawful alignments, greatly respecting the authority of kings and other rulers. Though no real stigma is attached to divine worship, due to their past Atlanteans are often maltheistic. Atlanteans that do pursue religion often venerate gods of crafting, knowledge, and the sea.

Adventurers: Atlantean adventures are very rare. Atlanteans that take up adventuring often do so in pursuit of exotic knowledge, rare materials, or perhaps even means of restoring their civilization to premience.

Male Names: Akakios, Demosthenes, Kallinikos, Philokrates, Solon, Timaios, Zotikos

Female Names: Agathe, Doris, Euphemia, Ligeia, Myrto, Sophia, Xanthippe
source:
Akashic Tales: Relics of Atlantis, pgs. 10–13
Akashic Tales: Hallow Icons, pg. 19

name: Avead (Retold)
racial_traits:
Standard Racial Traits

• Ability Score Modifiers: Avead are strong of both body and personality, but their passions' consistent impact upon their minds affects their willpower. They gain +2 Strength, +2 Charisma, and -2 Wisdom.

• Type: Avead are outsiders with the akashic and native subtypes. They also gain the racial subtype of their parents' race (such as the elf subtype if their parents were elves) and are treated as their parent race for the purposes of meeting prerequisites (such as feats or favored class bonuses).

• Size: Avead are Medium creatures and thus receive no bonuses or penalties due to their size.

• Speed: Avead have a base speed of 30 feet.

• Languages: Avead begin play speaking Common and one of the following languages: Abyssal, Aquan, Auran, Celestial, Draconic, Ignan, Infernal, Sylvan, or Terran. Avead with high Intelligence scores can also choose those languages as bonus languages. Alternatively; an avead can choose to use the languages of their parent race when determining their starting languages.

Defense Racial Traits

• Akashic Resistance: Avead are akashic creatures, granting them immunity to magical sleep effects.

• Emotional Resilience: Aveads are particularly resilient to effects that would attempt to alter their emotions. They gain a +2 bonus on Will saves against effects with the emotion or fear descriptors.

• Hardened Form: The flesh of an avead is hardened by their innate essence, granting them a +1 bonus to their natural armor.

Magical Racial Traits

• Innate Veil: Avead are always treated as if they had the Daevic Aspect veil shaped. This veil does not count against their normal limits on veils shaped, or their veil slots. They use their Charisma as their veilweaving modifier for this veil, and their character level as their veilweaving level. At 1st level, the avead chooses a single passion available to the daevic and treats that as their selected passion for their Daevic Aspect. If they later gain levels in the daevic class, they may choose whether to use their selected passion or their daevic passion with the veil, chosen when they first take a level in the class. This veil is formed directly from the avead's body, blood, and soul; it is not invisible when uninvested, and the avead cannot willingly suppress it or unshape it. It can still be suppressed through other means, such as by being dispelled or sundered. If it becomes unshaped through other means, it is automatically reshaped after one hour (and re-bound, if they have the ability to bind it) without requiring any actions on the avead's part.

• Innate Essence: Avead gain 1 point of essence.

• Passion-bound: Avead treat their veil granted by their innate veil racial feature as a passion veil if they take levels in the daevic class, allowing it to be affected by their class features that would affect their passion veils. If their passion modifies what ability score they use as their veilweaving modifier for their daevic veils, they may use that veilweaving modifier for their innate veil as well.

Senses Racial Traits

• Darkvision: Avead can see perfectly in the dark for up to 60 feet.

• Low-Light Vision: Avead can see twice as far as humans in conditions of dim light.

Akashic Racial Traits

All akashic creatures have the following racial traits unless noted otherwise:

• Essence Pool equal to racial hit die.

• Immunity to sleep effects.

• Low-light vision.

• Unless otherwise noted, akashic creatures always use Charisma as their primary veilweaving modifier. Akashic creatures with class levels use the primary veilweaving modifier of their class.

Alternate Racial Traits

The following alternate racial traits may be selected in place of one or more of the standard racial traits presented above.

• Born from the Smallfolk: Some avead are born from one of the races of the smallfolk, such as halflings or gnomes. These avead are Small creatures and gain a +1 size bonus to their AC, a +1 size bonus on attack rolls, a -1 penalty to their CMB and CMD, and a +4 size bonus to Stealth checks. Their base speed is also reduced to 20 feet. This trait replaces the avead's size and speed.

• Wildborn Bloodline: Some avead are born from the influence of a rarer form of daeva than those that commonly bond mortals and form daevic bonds. These avead are particularly unpredictable, embodying emotional aspects unknown among daevic bonds and using abilities far outside the norm. These avead do not gain the emotional resilience, hardened form or passionbound racial traits, but instead may choose any one veil to have shaped in place of the Daevic Aspect veil granted by their innate veil trait. This veil is chosen at 1st level, and cannot be changed. This veil can be of any slot, including esoteric chakra such as Blood or Ring. If a veil can be shaped in multiple chakra slots, the slot it is treated as shaped in is chosen at 1st level. This choice cannot be changed. This replaces the emotional resilience, hardened form and passion bound traits, and modifies the innate veil trait. A variant avead can take this alternate racial trait, replacing their altered passionbound and alternate innate veil traits instead of the base avead's, in addition to their emotional resistance and hardened form traits.

Variant Avead Heritages

Sometimes the daevic bloodline that influenced the avead's birth will have an even greater impact on them. These avead have dramatically different manifestations of their daeva heritage, even further resembling the appearance and abilities of the daeva whose bond their embody, as described on Table: Variant Avead Heritages.

Variant avead may still select alternate racial traits that replace abilities modified by their heritage.

Avead of one of these heritages have the following modifications:

• Ability Modifiers: Variant avead use the ability modifiers presented by their heritage in place of their normal ability score modifiers.

• Passion-bound: Variant avead only treat their innate veil as a passion veil if their heritage matches their daevic passion. For example, Justice-Born avead would only treat their innate veil as a passion veil if they take the Wrath passion.

• Veilweaving Modifier: The ability score used to determine the variant avead's veilweaving modifier for their innate veil.

• Alternate Innate Veil: At 1st level, a variant avead can choose to replace their Daevic Aspect veil with the listed alternate innate veil. It otherwise follows all other rules of their innate veil racial trait. If a veil can be shaped in multiple chakra slots, the slot it is treated as shaped in is chosen at 1st level. This choice cannot be changed. If the avead chooses to keep the Daevic Aspect veil, they must choose their associated passion when shaping it. If the avead gains levels in the daevic class with a different passion, they choose which of those two passions to gain the benefits of when they shape the veil.
description:
Occasionally when a mortal with a daeva bonded to them conceives a child that bond influences their bloodline going into the future, passing some of the bonded daeva's power along to their offspring or distant descendents. These beings become suffused with that daeva's akashic powers, becoming a being that isn't quite mortal and isn't quite daeva. Avead (pronounced ah-ved) often possess traits of their daeva bloodline above and beyond their altered appearance, often having a personality tinged by their daeva bloodline's emotional or passion requirements. This natural alignment to an emotion or passion often draws daeva of that aspect to them, causing many avead to form daeva bonds themselves.

Physical Appearance: Avead are born looking identical to their birth species, the power of daeva blood within their body not fully manifesting until they reach puberty. At such a point, their body begins to change as their powers manifest and their innate veil takes physical form. Due the the wild differences in the appearances of daeva they draw the effects of their blood from, these physical changes can be very minor, from slightly odd eye or hair colors such as ruby red, or changes that would cause the avead to stand out distinctly, such as wildly different skin pigmentation, horns, strange eyes, or altered hands or limbs. Their innate veil also differentiates them from others of their birth species; their innate veils take on a physical form unlike those shaped by veilweavers. Each avead's innate veil manifests differently, and rarely follows the standard appearance of their veil. An avead's Daevic Aspect attuned to Desire may appear as a pair of faintly glowing wings or energy emitted from their back, as an example.

Society: Avead appear so infrequently that the given number in any society is so low that they rarely form unique societal structures amongst only avead. Avead will generally integrate into their birth society, or be shunned and ostracized and forced into a life of solitude. Because avead often live their young life without knowledge of their birthright, they tend to pick up the mannerisms and societal expectations of their birth society, even if they are later shunned once their powers and physical alterations manifest. In societies where daeva are revered or daeva bonds are particularly common, avead are often revered as blessed by the daeva and often find themselves in positions of religious or political power.

Relations: Within more traditional or conservative communities, avead can face a significant amount of prejudice or ostracisation due to their strange or alien appearances and innately dangerous abilities. In such cases, they tend to seek out communities that will accept them, often those formed up of other ostracized folk such as tieflings, half-elves, or half-orcs. Those rare avead who congregate to form communities of other avead rarely last more than a single generation, the effects of daeva magic on their bloodline rarely being passed down to their children, though for their brief lifespans those communities often thrive and produce all manner of akashic discoveries.

Alignment and Religion: Though an avead's natural attunement to a specific state of emotion can have an affect on their actions, it rarely affects their alignment or choice of religion. Avead will generally follow the same religions of their birth society, though sometimes a particularly bigoted or ostracising community may drive the avead to forgo a religion they were born in to.

Adventurers: Due to the high rate of abandonment after their powers and appearance changes manifest, many avead are left without a true home once they reach adulthood. Many of these avead use their innate powers for adventuring in a hope to find themselves or to find a new place they can call home.

Names: Avead are often named based on the society they were born into. Human-born avead would have human names, elven-born avead would have elven names, and so on. Sometimes an avead will adopt a new name based on their passion or their appearance after their powers manifest.

name: Cactusfolk
racial_traits:
• +2 Dexterity, +2 Wisdom, -2 Strength: Cactusfolk are quick and half an innate affinity for the natural world, but their small frames are lacking in physical power.

• Small: Cactusfolk are Small and gain a +1 size bonus to AC and attack rolls as well as a +4 size bonus on Stealth checks, but they take a -1 penalty to their CMB and CMD.

• High Speed: Cactusfolk have a base land speed of 40 feet.

• Low-light Vision: Cactusfolk see twice as far as a human in areas of dim light.

• Bonus Essence: Cactusfolk are wellsprings of natural energy and start play with 1 bonus essence point in their pool.

• Essence-Born: Cactusfolk are plants with the akashic subtype, though their surprisingly humanoid physiology means they lack the immunities to mind-affecting effects, paralysis, poison, polymorph, and stunning that the plant type usually grants.

• Desert Strider: A cactusfolk moves through nonmagical difficult terrain in desert environments at normal speed.

• Sleep Immunity: Cactusfolk are immune to magical sleep effects.

• Spines (Ex): Cactusfolk grow spines they can use to attack with and defend themselves. A cactusfolk’s base damage with their unarmed strikes is 1d6, they are always considered armed, and they can deal lethal damage with their unarmed strikes. Their unarmed strikes always deal bludgeoning and piercing damage. Once per day when a cactusfolk is damaged by an enemy within 30 feet, they can unleash a spray of needles at that enemy as a ranged touch attack dealing 1d6 piercing damage with a x4 critical multiplier. Cactusfolk can invest essence into this ability to improve its effects.
• Essence: For each point of essence invested in this ability, the cactusfolk gains a +1 insight bonus to attack and damage rolls with their unarmed strikes, and their spray of needles deals an additional 1d6 piercing damage.

• Languages: Cactusfolk begin play speaking Common and Sylvan. Cactusfolk with high Intelligence scores can choose from the following languages: Draconic, Elven, Ignan, Giant, Gnome, and Terran.

Akashic Racial Traits

All akashic creatures have the following racial traits unless noted otherwise:

• Essence Pool equal to racial hit die.

• Immunity to sleep effects.

• Low-light vision.

• Unless otherwise noted, akashic creatures always use Charisma as their primary veilweaving modifier. Akashic creatures with class levels use the primary veilweaving modifier of their class.
description:
Cactusfolk are small but quick creatures. Reminiscent of a bipedal cactus, cactusfolk are protected by a coat of prickly spines.

Physical Description: Cactusfolk stand just over 3 feet tall. Their compact bodies feature rubbery green skin and numerous long, thin spines that cover them from head to toe. Cactusfolk often feature bright flowers, typically yellow but potentially appearing in almost any color imaginable, which grow from their heads, backs, or shoulders.

Society: Cactusfolk are quiet creatures and rarely live in communities of their own kind. Indeed, the name "cactusfolk" is not a word used by the species; they have no word for themselves as a collective group since their worldview is much more focused on individual growth and expression over group categorization. Ever curious, individuals or small groups of cactusfolk occasionally bond with members of other species and live alongside them. Cactusfolk reproduce through pollination, and any two cactusfolk are capable of reproducing with one another.

Relations: While some hunters from other species view cactusfolk as exotic pets or even unique garden accents, most species that encounter cactusfolk find them to be charming and wise, if somewhat naive. Morphbears often integrate small colonies of cactusfolk into their communities, maintaining hives of honey-wasps that help the cactusfolk pollinate and reproduce. Honey-wasps also eat the harmful parasites that feast on cactusfolk flesh and flowers, and can be found naturally in most areas where cactusfolk make their homes.

Alignment and Religion: Cactusfolk favor neutral alignments, but they frequently mimic and ultimately adopt the behaviors and alignment of other creatures they befriend. Cactusfolk communities typically worship whichever constellation is most conveniently viewed from their communities. Their desert homes often have strong associations with fire signs, and cactusfolk zodiacs who channel Phoenix frequently act as the councilors and healers of their communities.

Adventurers: Cactusfolk lack adrenaline and similar biological imperatives that often drive humanoids and mammalian species to seek a life of adventure. Most often, cactusfolk adventurers see themselves as scholars, observing the actions of a small group of friends who make their living seeking out treasure and fighting monsters, though occasionally an event of significant import can also lead a cactusfolk to undertake a dangerous journey in pursuit of knowledge or aid.
source: Akashic Realms Volume 2: The Quiet Lands, pgs. 6–7

name: Creeper
racial_traits:
• +2 Strength, +2 Charisma, -2 Dexterity: Slow and deliberate, creepers are full of quiet power.

• Small: Creepers are Small and gain a +1 size bonus to AC and attack rolls as well as a +4 size bonus on Stealth checks, but they take a -1 penalty to their CMB and CMD.

• Slow Speed: Creepers have short legs, even for a Small species, and thick, slow-moving blood. They have a base speed of 20 feet.

• Bonus Essence: Creepers draw steady streams of essence to their focused wills and start play with 1 bonus essence point in their pool.

• Essence-Born: Creepers are plants with the akashic subtype, though their surprisingly humanoid physiology means they lack the immunities to mind-affecting effects, paralysis, poison, polymorph, and stunning that the plant type usually grants.

• Lantern Eyes: Creeper’s eyes glow with a sickly yellow light in the dark, granting them darkvision out to 60 feet and a +2 racial bonus to Intimidate checks while in areas of dim light or darkness.

• Sleep Immunity: Creepers are immune to magical sleep effects.

• Slasher: Creepers are particularly proficient with small stabbing and cutting weapons like knives. Whenever a creeper deals damage with a light or one-handed weapon that deals either piercing or slashing damage, they deal an additional 1 point of precision damage. A creeper may invest essence into this ability to improve its effects.
• Essence: For each point of essence invested into this ability, the creeper deals an additional 1d6 points of precision damage when attacking with a light or one-handed piercing or slashing weapon.

• Languages: Creepers begin play speaking Common and Sylvan. Creepers with high Intelligence scores can choose from the following languages: Draconic, Elven, Giant, Gnome, and Terran.

Akashic Racial Traits

All akashic creatures have the following racial traits unless noted otherwise:

• Essence Pool equal to racial hit die.

• Immunity to sleep effects.

• Low-light vision.

• Unless otherwise noted, akashic creatures always use Charisma as their primary veilweaving modifier. Akashic creatures with class levels use the primary veilweaving modifier of their class.
description:
Creepers are slow, deadly, and possessed of an unusual air of menace. Appearing as a cross between a plant and some kind of tailed turtle, these solitary creatures are greatly feared by those who have witnessed their deadly skills in battle.

Physical Description: Creepers grow to be just under 4 feet tall. A hard shell of bark-like matter covers their hunched backs and serves as protection from wouldbe predators. Luminous eyes help cut throughout the darkened caves and canopied bogs of their primary homelands. Compact, corded fibers supply their short limbs with strength akin to crashing trees and rolling stones.

Society: Creepers tend toward solitary lives when young adults but often seek out a community as they reach a maturity and self-awareness. Surprisingly this community does not necessarily have to be one of other creepers; rather each creeper tends toward advisory, monitoring, or other ancillary roles to a larger group of nearly any species or people. This may stem from their ability to reproduce by spores and thus not requiring others of their species to continue their kind. Despite asexual reproduction creepers still present vestigial genders.

Relations: Creepers are inclined to live in small communities of like-minded individuals. Some say this is from a deep distrust in all other living things while others attribute this to a fear of the self, that they will harm those they come to love. Historical examples support both ideas and perhaps the truth lies between them. Of the other species common to the Quiet Lands, wolgers are most frequently allied with creeper folk. Often times a single Cabal of sages will support one or more tribes of wolger. Ancestral rivalry between the wyrmkin and creepers has been a source of atrocities on both sides. Tensions are high between the two people.

Alignment and Religion: Creepers tend toward extremes of a singular aligned aspect being either focused or passionate toward a singular end. Creepers hold great reverence for the Pasha of Stone who guided the Guardians to create the Caves of Ryatha and elevated the creepers awareness of the cosmos. Ancient scraps of lore tie them to the Zealous Fortress but this connection is all but the vaguest of apocrypha in the current age. Few species bear the Brionithic Empire enmity as much as the creepers.

Adventurers: Creepers understand the concepts of fate, duty, and obligation. Many of them make their way into the Multiverse under one of more of these burdens. Those that reach Hyraeatan often find themselves enmeshed with the Foreseen or sword the Hands of Onus. Rarer still a visitor to the Caves of the Zoadar informs a young creeper that they are to leave with them as advisor or companion.
source: Akashic Realms Volume 2: The Quiet Lands, pgs. 9–10

name: Daitya
racial_traits:
• Ability Score Modifiers: +2 Wisdom, +2 Strength, –2 Dexterity: Daitya are strong of will and body, but not too agile.

• Size: Daitya are Medium creatures and have no bonuses or penalties due to their size.

• Type: Daityas count as both human and humanoid (giant) for any effect related to race. They also have the akashic subtype.

• Speed: Daityas have a base speed of 30 feet.

• Vision: Daityas have low-light vision (they can see twice as far as humans in conditions of dim light.)

• Akashic Immunity: As akashic creatures, Daitya are immune to magical sleep effects. They also gain a +2 racial saving throw bonus against any spell or effect that would modify or erase their memories.

• Mountaineer (Su): The Daitya gain a +1 insight bonus on initiative checks and Climb, Knowledge (geography), Perception, Stealth, and Survival skill checks when they are in mountainous terrain. A Daitya traveling through mountains leaves only a faint trail and is difficult to track, adding +5 to the DC. The Daitya can invest essence into this ability; for each point of essence invested, the insight bonus increases by +1 and the tracking DC by +2. They also move through non-magical difficult terrain in mountain environments at normal speed.

• Powerful Build: The physical stature of Daityas lets them function in many ways as if they were one size category larger. Whenever a Daitya is subject to a size modifier or special size modifier for a Combat Maneuver Bonus or Combat Maneuver Defense (such as during grapple checks, bull rush attempts, and trip attempts), the Daitya is treated as one size larger if doing so is advantageous to him. A Daitya is also considered to be one size larger when determining whether a creature's special attacks based on size (such as grab or swallow whole) can affect them. A Daitya can use weapons designed for a creature one size larger without penalty. However, their space and reach remain those of a creature of the actual size. The benefits of this racial trait stack with the effects of powers, abilities, and spells that change the subject's size category.

• Racial Veils: A Daitya capable of shaping veils adds the Danava's Gada and Danava's Angarkha to their veil list.

• Bonus Essence: Daitya have a natural connection to the unique power of akasha and start play with 1 bonus essence point in their pool.

• Languages: Daityas begin play speaking Giant. Daitya with high Intelligence scores can choose from the following: Aquan, Common and Draconic.

Alternate Racial Traits

Devastator (Su): Daitya have developed a reduced version of one of the danava's abilities. Once per round, when attacking with a bludgeoning weapon, a Daitya with this ability ignores 1 point of damage reduction or hardness for their first attack of the round. The Daitya can invest essence into this ability; for each point of essence invested, ignore one more point of damage reduction or hardness. This replaces mountaineer.

Iron Resilience (Ex): Daitya have developed a reduced version of one of the danava's abilities. Once per round as an immediate action, a Daitya can reduce the damage from one attack they take by a number of points equal to their Strength modifier (minimum 1) by taking a point of essence burn. This replaces mountaineer.

Water Magic (Su): Your people's rapport with water is stronger in you. You gain the create water orison as an at will spell–like ability. Any spell with the water descriptor cast by you is treated as if your caster level was one higher. This ability can only be taken by a character whose 1st character level is in a caster class. This replaces bonus essence.

Water Memories (Su): The bodies of some Daitya have lingering ties to the Akashic Records. They may shape the Marid's Sanadils veil. This veil does not count against their normal limits on veils shaped, or their veil slots. Daitya use their Wisdom modifier as their veilweaving modifier for this veil, and their character level as their veilweaving level. This replaces mountaineer.
description:
Physical Description: Commonly standing over seven feet tall, Daitya tower over most of the medium races. The males and females have strong, heavily muscled bodies, with the males having thick facial hair and preferring mustaches. Their skin ranges from bright red to a dark brown, while their hair is almost always black with the rare dark gray. Their eyes typically range from light to dark gray, but a small number have completely white eyes. Most Daitya tend to wear clothes made from giant goat skin, with only a couple of them using clothes made of fabric, bought from merchants down the mountains.

Society: The Daitya are very few, numbering no more than ten thousand members and having only one homeland. There, they have a clan structure with each member contributing to the benefit of the clan. Their homeland is so inhospitable that to this date they have had little contact with other races, with only the few merchants that come to the mountain's slope to trade. The few Daitya that venture outside do it because they feel like they don't fit with their kin, or because they want to know about the world.

Relations: While not xenophobic, the fake memories of the Daitya have made them distrustful, and they have never invited a foreigner to their land. Those who venture the world tend to have open minds, free of racial preconceptions, and tend to get along with all races.

Alignment and Religion: The Daitya have a few druids, shamans and oracles that venerate the lake and work as priests for their people. As a side effect of drinking the danava's tears, they tend to follow the clan's rules to the letter, and tend to be lawful,either good or neutral. There have been some rare evil individuals, but even they have been more numerous than the almost non-existent chaotic ones.

Adventurers: Most Daitya found outside their land are adventurers, with a thirst for knowledge about the rest of the vast world. They quickly learn to value money, although it rarely becomes their purpose in life. They tend to become akashic barbarians, monks, fighters or rangers, or develop their watery ties with the world by becoming druids, oracles or shamans. A few become radiants or gurus. Bards and skalds Daitya are not unheard of, but rogues are very rare. Most akashic classes have been represented among the Daitya, although all Daevic but Grief are incredibly rare.

Male Names: Aadhesh, Bhimasura, Dadhmo, Nandur, Virokana

Female Names: Holika, Jalandhara, Kesi, Rahuna, Simhika

name: Datum
ability_modifiers: no Strength score, -4 Constitution, +2 Intelligence, +2 Wisdom
racial_traits:
Sidebar: Races Without Strength Scores

Generating ability scores for most of the races follow the standard methods (standard, classic, heroic, dice pool, or purchase). Races without Strength scores are an exception, and require some slight changes to the ability score generation methods. The changes are as follows, corresponding to the five standard methods. Standard: Roll 4d6, discarding the lowest result as normal, and sum the results, but only do this five times, and assign them as you see fit, skipping Strength.
• Classic: Roll 3d6 and sum the results five times, and assign them as you see fit, skipping Strength.

• Heroic: Roll 2d6 and add 6 to the sum of each. Do this five times and assign them as you see fit, skipping Strength.

• Dice Pool: Instead of a pool of 24d6, races without Strength get a pool of 20d6 to assign to the ability scores, except for Strength. These characters still must assign a minimum of 3d6 in each of the other ability scores. Increase the number for high-powered games.

• Purchase: When using the purchase method for ability scores, assume members of races without Strength have a Strength score of 10 and buy the rest of the abilities normally using the points allocated for the campaign's power level.

• Ability Scores: Datum are known for both their keen intellects and their mental fortitude, but their odd nature makes their forms both nimble and somewhat fragile. Datum characters gain +2 Wis, +2 Int, –4 Con. Datum do not have a Strength score as described in their quasi-real trait (see "Sidebar: Races without Strength Scores" for rules on generating their ability scores).

• Child of the Lattice: Datum are humanoids with the akashic and datum subtypes.

• Tiny: Datum are tiny creatures and thus gain a +2 size bonus to their AC, a +2 size bonus on attack rolls, a –2 penalty on combat maneuver checks and to their CMD, and a +8 size bonus on Stealth checks. Datum take up a space of 2-1/2 feet by 2-1/2 feet, so up to four of these characters can fit into a single square. Datum have a natural reach of 0 feet, meaning they can’t reach into adjacent squares. They must enter an opponent’s square to attack it in melee. This provokes an attack of opportunity from the opponent. Since they have no natural reach, they do not threaten the squares around them. Other creatures can move through those squares without provoking attacks of opportunity. Like most tiny creatures, datum cannot flank an enemy.

• Speed: Datum have a base speed of 20 feet and a fly speed of 10 ft. (perfect).

• Semi-Artificial: Datum were not born of natural processes, or even the whims of the gods, but instead are a byproduct of failed mystical and technological planar tampering. For the purposes of effects targeting creatures by type (such as a ranger’s favored enemy and bane weapons), datum count as both humanoids and constructs(with the robot subtype). Datum have a +4 racial bonus on all saving throws against paralysis and stun effects, and are not subject to fatigue or exhaustion. Datum are immune to disease, poison, and infestation effects that do not originate from other datum, creatures with the virtual creature template or the incorporeal subtype, or from objects that have been transformed into virtual matter (such as by their racial convert item ability). They also do not need to eat, drink, or breathe except as described in tethered virtual form. Due to being akashic creatures datum are also immune to sleep effects.

• Vulnerability to Disruption: Electrical discharges and bursts of force can disrupt a datum’s form. All datum have vulnerability to electricity and force damage, taking 50% more damage from those types.

• Quasi-Real: A datum's form is similar in many respects to an incorporeal creature. It is not flesh and blood, but instead a coherent conglomeration of energy and the strange virtual matter of the Lattice. However, via this hologramlike manifestation a datum can interact weakly with the physical world. Consequently, physical objects and forces can disrupt and alter their forms in return. Datum have the following properties:

• Datum only take three quarters damage from a corporeal source (minimum 1). Corporeal spells and effects that do not cause damage only have a 75% chance of affecting a datum. Ghost touch weapons, ectoplasmic spells and similar effects, and electricity and force damage and effects affect a datum normally.

• Corporeal creatures and objects targeted by a datum’s damaging spells, veils, and other effects only take half damage. A datum’s spells and effects that do not cause damage only have a 50% chance of affecting corporeal creatures and objects. Ectoplasmic spells and similar effects, force descriptor spells and effects, used by the datum affect other creatures normally. Similarly, spells and effects used by the datum against corporeal creatures and objects that are in contact with their host, or effects they target their host with, function normally.

• Datum can not gain natural armor bonuses.

• A datum can enter or pass through solid objects, but the datum must maintain line of sight with their host. A datum cannot pass through a force or electrical effect.

• Datum can pass through and operate in water as easily as they do in air, subject to the limits of their host. Datum can fall, but not beyond the range of their tethered virtual form ability unless their host is falling with them. However, they only take 75% of the normal falling damage. They also cannot make trip or grapple attacks against corporeal creatures, nor can they be tripped or grappled by them. In fact, they cannot take any physical action that would move or manipulate a corporeal opponent or its equipment, nor are they normally subject to such actions except in cases where their host is targeted. The exception to this would be against other datum or creatures with the virtual creature template.

• Datum have no weight and do not set off traps that are triggered by weight.

• Datum move silently and cannot be heard with Perception checks if they don’t wish to be. They do not have a Strength score, so their Dexterity modifier applies to their melee attacks, ranged attacks, and CMB. Most non-visual senses, such as scent and blindsight, are either ineffective or only partly effective with regard to datum. Though it is possible to detect their host via such senses.

• When a datum dies their remains return to the Lattice after a number of hours equal to the datum's hit dice. Magical effects that would stop decay such as gentle repose also delay this. Their remains are still tethered to their host while they are in this state.

• Tethered Virtual Form: A datum’s presence in the physical world is anchored and stabilized by technological objects referred to as hosts which act as umbilicals to their home realm. A datum can change their host as a full-round action. They can select an unattended technological object, or an attended technological object that is in the possession of a willing creature, within close range (25 +5 ft. per 2 HD). Datum project themselves from their host object in a hologramlike form. This form functions as described in their quasireal racial trait. The maximum range they can move from their current host is close range (25 +5 ft. per 2 HD) .

The properties of the connection between a datum and their host area as follows:
• A datum is always considered to be attending their host, useless if it is already attended by another creature. They can activate, deactivate, target, or otherwise use it as if they were in physical contact with it. They can not however normally move their host, unless it is an object that is capable of being made to move under its own power.

• A host must have a size category of at least diminutive, and can normally only serve as the host form a single datum. At the GM’s discretion an exceptionally large or complex technological object (e.g. a technological crafting laboratory or spaceship) can serve as the host for multiple datum. If the datum’s host is a technological object that requires charges, or grants charges, it must have at least one charge remaining in order for it to be used as a host. Technological objects that are expended when used (e.g. pharmaceuticals, grenades, ect.) can not be used as hosts.

• Effects that would move a datum beyond the range of this ability (e.g. teleportation, reposition combat maneuvers) automatically fail (at GM discretion such effects can move the datum to the edge of this range instead), but effects that move a datum’s host move the datum as well. Their distance from their host does not change. The datum does not get a saving throw against being moved with their host, but depending on the source of the movement their host might get a saving throw to resist the movement.

• Realms other than the Lattice can not sustain a datum. If their host is destroyed, exhausts its last charge, or this ability is suppressed, the datum is treated as a being in an airless environment and the normal rules for suffocation apply. A datum can not move from their current square while in this state, but they can gain a new host as long as a suitable object is in range. This can not be prevented in any way other than by gaining a new host.

A datum's senses are the same as a normal human (with the addition of low-light vision due to the akashic subtype), regardless of any cameras, microphones, or other mechanical sensory devices their host may possess.

• Inviolate Nature: Even though datum can be affected by polymorph effects, their nature as data based lifeforms transcends the shape they take. Datum normally retain their convert item, quasi-real, and tethered virtual form racial abilities while under the effects of a polymorph effect. However, if such an effect would change them into a form that is of the following types they lose the ability to retrieve items from, or store items in, their inventory: animal, dragon, elemental, magical beast, plant, or vermin.

• Planar Interface (Su): Datum can use their innate connection to the Lattice to form a mental link with a single technological item (either in their possession or unattended), or a willing construct with the robot subtype, they can see within 25 ft. as a standard action. This ability can be invested with essence. For each point of essence invested in this ability, its range increases by 10 ft.

The datum can target with spells and effects, activate, or otherwise access or use technological items or creatures they are linked to in this way as if they were in physical contact with them. For example, a datum helmsman could use mending on their bonded mech without the effect being reduced by their quasi-real trait if they formed this kind of connection with it. Similarly, they could attempt a Disable Device check to open an electronic lock that they have targeted with this ability.

If an object leaves the range of this ability then this mental link is broken, otherwise a mental link can be broken as a free action on the datum’s turn. Effects that would prevent dimensional movement (e.g. dimensional anchor) also prevent the use of this ability.

• Convert Item (Su): In a process that takes an hour, a datum can convert an unattended physical item into a form that they can use. They must be in the same square as the item they are converting for the entire duration of the process. At the end of this process the item becomes a quasi-real object composed of virtual matter from the Lattice and resizes to fit or be comfortably handled by the datum (usually). They can only convert items that are intended for use by a creature up to two size categories larger than themself (e.g. a sword intended for a human or a backpack intended for an elf). They can also convert raw materials intended for crafting into a virtual form in this way at a rate equal to their maximum carrying capacity for converted items per hour. A datum can not use an item that has undergone this process as a host.

Datum are not normally capable of reversing this process. However, a converted item can be targeted with fabricate to return it to physical form and its original size. This method can also be used to transform an item that was crafted by a datum from converted materials into a normal physical object.

A converted item that the datum does not decide to equip or hold can be stored in an extra-dimensional space within their form known as their “inventory” unless they decide to let it drop to the ground. Their inventory does not negate the “weight” of items and materials, but does negate their volume. An inventory can hold a maximum number of pounds of items, or raw materials, up to the datum’s carrying maximum capacity. A datum can store or remove objects from this space as a move action. A datum uses Intelligence instead of Strength for the purposes of determining carrying capacity with regard to converted items and materials, and take encumbrance penalties as if they were physically carrying an item stored in their inventory.

Datum can freely exchange such converted items and materials among themselves and creatures with the virtual creature template in the same manner as normal creatures could trade items. They can also move, manipulate, or otherwise interact with them, using Intelligence in place of Strength, the same way that most creatures interact with normal objects. Additionally, datum can attempt to steal an item stored in another datum’s or virtual creature's inventory as if it were a backpack or similar worn container. Converted worn and held items can similarly be stolen or disarmed.

Otherwise, converted items can not be stolen or otherwise forcefully taken.

Items that are dropped, lost, or discarded by a datum remain in their current location for a number of hours equal to its hardness (minimum 1 hour) before vanishing into the Lattice unless they are taken into the possession of a datum or similar creature.

When a datum dies any objects in their inventory, or otherwise on their person that have been converted, are lost along with their remains to the Lattice after a number of hours equal to the datum's hit dice.

• Natural Technologist: Due to their innate connection to technology, datum gain Technologist as a bonus feat.

• Bonus Essence: Datum have a natural connection to the unique power of akasha and start play with 1 bonus essence point in their pool.

• First Host: All datum begin play with a physical technological object suitable for use as a host that has a cost of up 100 gp. Batteries, basic naniteweave bodysuits, e-picksTG (black), and lev-torches are common options. Such items start with a full charge if applicable.

• Languages: Datum begin play speaking Common and Datum. Datum with high Intelligence scores can choose any languages they want (except secret languages, such as Druidic).

Alternate Racial Traits

• Divine Code: When certain datum first emerge from the Lattice they sometimes catch the attention of a machine lifeform that has ascended to divinity, the Lattice itself, or occasionally other divine powers. The datum selects a single deity whose domain's include Akasha, Artifice, Knowledge, or Technology. The datum gains a faintly luminous mark somewhere on their body, usually on a hand, that is in the shape of this deity's holy symbol. Additionally, they always treat Knowledge (religion) as a class skill and can cast guidance as a spell-like ability at-will as a full-round action. This replaces natural technologist.

• Hacker: The datum gains Hacker as a bonus feat. This replaces natural technologist.

• Inherent Format: The datum gains Acquire Format as a bonus feat. This replaces natural technologist.

• Magical Hosts: Under the idea that any significantly advanced magic is indistinguishable from technology some datum have learned to maintain their link to the Lattice via magical items. Instead of technological items the datum must select magical items as their host and starts play with an ioun torch or other magical item with a cost of up to 100 gp. Any magical item that the datum selects as their host must be a permanent magic item or a charged magic item that has at least 1 charge remaining. This trait alters tethered virtual form and replaces the first host ability.
description:
The datum are a race born of serendipity and hubris. In an age now lost, on a world who's advancement was such that all its people were like unto gods, a brilliant vizier wanted more. Emperor Mairus of the Seven Suns decided that he should bodily ascend into the Akashic Record, usurp a portion of its nature, and become a living nexus for all digital information in the universe. Giving him dominion over all data, technology, and more. The ritual he crafted and the technologies he developed to this end sundered him and bathed his home world in akashic fire. His deeds created a permanent scar in the Akashic Record that if observed would look like crystalline circuitry branching out in three dimensions, but he succeeded after a fashion. Mairus' ruin birthed a new realm, known as the Lattice.

The datum themselves are in large part a conglomeration of digital information, akashic energies, and the remnants of the souls of Marius' people. When found within their home realm, they seem very much like any other technologically advanced humanoid civilization. However, datum that are encountered in the rest of the universe are oftentimes conceptualized as a "naturally" occurring cousin to artificial intelligences. This misunderstanding of their nature is not entirely unreasonable.

Physical Description: Datum can bear a superficial similarity to any humanoid race depending on the nature of the data and souls that were involved in their birth. Albeit the faintly glowing circuitry-like markings covering their forms, and the natural translucency of their projected quasi-real forms mark them as being something other than tiny humanoids.

In their native realm they appear with a size that matches their apparent race, but they most commonly manifest as tiny in size outside of the Lattice.

Society: Innovation is the beating heart of datum society. This is true both within the Lattice and in the few datum communities that have established themselves on other planes. Innovation in this case is not necessarily technological, though it often is. Datum seek to push the boundaries of most fields including art, and even political theory. Though datum can integrate into most societies, they tend to gravitate towards those with a democratic structure, and datum society in the Lattice is governed by an elected council.

Relations: Datum that travel outside of the Lattice are naturally curious about many things including other races. They tend to have the strongest affinity for members of the race whose likeness they bear. They also tend to be fond of androids due feeling a kinship for them as another ensouled technological race. In general when living among other races datum form symbiotic relationships with their neighbors. They often offer their mastery of technology and other abilities to support their fellows in exchange for the defense of their host devices.

Alignment and Religion: Though good and evil are rarely major considerations for the datum, causing them to lean towards neutrality, they do tend towards extremes of law and chaos. This is due to the fact that on the whole they either give in to their natural curiosity to an extreme, or they become a reflection of the orderly nature of their home realm.

In terms of religion, most datum venerate their home realm, the Lattice. Though a minority do worship other akashic and technological deities.

Adventurers: Datum adventurers are somewhat rare, due in part to the discomfort and risk involved with existing outside of their home realm. The rare few datum that do are often those whose data is derived from that of naturally adventurous races, those that wish to fix or improve the rest of the universe, or those that have been exiled for various reasons such as political dissent or for committing grave offenses. Datum that do find their way to other planes of existence tend towards classes with companion creatures or mechs such as the druid or helmsman, or support classes namely bards and radiants. Datum clerics and oracles are also relatively common.

Names: Datum names generally are very similar to those common to the race that spawned them, though they generally have random seeming strings of characters attached to their names the same way that may races have family names (e.g. Anabel -Q1W.2 “stroke Q One W dot Two”, and Z?45! “Zed question Forty-Five exclamation” Gadreel)

name: Deva-Asura
ability_modifiers: +2 Constitution, +2 Charisma, -2 to One Ability Score
racial_traits:
• Ability Score Modifiers: +2 Cha, +2 Con, -2 to any other ability score of their choice. Deva-asuras are known for the force of their personalities and their exceptional health. However, they also inherit some of the flaws of their progenitor.

• Size: Deva-asuras are Medium creatures and have no bonuses or penalties due to their size.

• Type: Deva-asuras are outsiders with the akashic and native subtypes.

• Low-Light Vision: Deva-asuras can see twice as far as humans in conditions of dim light.

• Akashic Immunity: Deva-asuras are akashic creatures, granting them immunity to magical sleep effects.

• Shadow of Divinity: Though deva-asuras do not possess true divine omnipotence they do bear its mark. Deva-asuras gain the ability to shape the Enfleshed Omnipotence veil. This veil does not count against their normal limits on veils shaped, or their veil slots. They use their highest mental ability score’s modifier as their veilweaving modifier for this veil, and their character level as their veilweaving level. As a supernatural effect, mundane and magical clothing or armor automatically alters or adjusts itself while worn by the Deva-Asura to fit the additional arms granted to them by this veil. Additionally, they also gain 1 point of essence.

• Divine Inviolability: Deva-asuras gain a +2 racial bonus on Fortitude saves against disease and poison, including magical diseases.

• Inherited Grace: At creation all deva-asuras select a single oracle mystery that is associated with the deity or cosmic force that they arose from. They gain a single revelation from this mystery that could be selected by a 1st level oracle. When determining the effects of the selected revelation, they are treated as having an effective oracle level equal to their character level -3 (minimum 1). This ability does not allow them to count as having the mystery or revelation class feature for the purposes of fulfilling prerequisites.

Deva-asuras that have levels in the oracle class may use their oracle level or their character level, whichever is higher, to determine the effects of the revelation granted by this ability. Additionally, if the mystery selected by them as a part of this ability matches the mystery they select at oracle level 1st, their oracle level is treated as 1 higher for the purposes of determining the effects of the revelation gained from this ability.

• Ageless: Deva-asuras cease aging naturally once they reach adulthood and cannot die of old age. Until they reach adulthood they age at the same rate as their mortal parent(s) if any or the race most common in the place of their birth. Spells and effects that cause aging affect a deva-asura as normal, but they have a +2 racial bonus on saving throws against such effects.

• Pluripotent Talents: Deva-Asura chooses two favored classes at 1st level. However, one of the classes selected by the deva-asura must be a class that grants an essence pool.

Alternate Racial Traits

Divine Bearing: The deva-asura gains +2 racial bonus to Diplomacy and Intimidate. This bonus is doubled against outsiders with the extraplanar subtype. This trait replaces divine inviolability.

Divine Emanation: Deva-asura are generally resistant to mortal ailments like poison or disease, but a few have a godlike emanation that protects them from the unnatural, gaining a +1 sacred bonus against divine spells and spell-like and supernatural abilities of outsiders. This ability replaces divine inviolability.

Echo of Aspects: The deva-asura gains the ability to shape the Manyfold Aspect veil. This veil does not count against their normal limits on veils shaped, or their veil slots. They use their highest mental ability score’s modifier as their veilweaving modifier for this veil, and their character level as their veilweaving level. As a supernatural effect, mundane and magical clothing or armor automatically alters or adjusts itself while worn by the Deva-Asura to accommodate the additional heads granted to them by this veil. This trait replaces shadow of divinity.

Trace of Enlightenment: Sometimes the enlightenment of a deva-asura’s progenitor or the cosmic circumstances of their birth leaves an indelible mark on their being. The deva-asura gains the ability to shape the Ajna of the Guru. This veil does not count against their normal limits on veils shaped, or their veil slots. They use their highest mental ability score’s modifier as their veilweaving modifier for this veil, and their character level as their veilweaving level. This trait replaces shadow of divinity.

Incorrupt: The deva-asura gains a +2 racial bonus on saving throws to prevent its essence from being corrupted by other creatures or effects. This trait replaces pluripotent talents.
description:
Physical Description: Either born of a mortal parent as babies or born fully formed in their teens, the deva-asura look like the mortals from the surroundings. They always look like perfect specimens of their parent species, but more often than not, they always have something that marks them as different from their peers: a birthmark in the shape of a holy symbol, an unnatural eye color, metallic hair or skin, or even just a regal aura of uniqueness about them. However, when they come of age, they manifest their Shadow of Divinity (or any variant of it), and depending on their community they can be treated as divine creatures, or monsters of the worst kind.

Society: No matter their origin, deva-asura are destined for greatness. They quickly become folk-heroes, leaders or even famous awe-inspiring masters. But even while they make allies and friends easily, they also make rivals and enemies as often. Even their deaths are grandiose.

Relations: Deva-asura are seen both with respect and fear. Even among people that share their likeness, they tend to be seen as not belonging. They get along better with other mortals touched by outside forces, like aasimars, tieflings and jinnborn, as well as other misunderstood individuals like half-orcs or vishkanya.

Alignment and Religion: While deva-asura can be of any alignment, they tend to be passionate individuals, and as such are rarely neutral or true neutral.

Adventurers: If there is something all deva-asura share is a penchant for power. All of them, even the youngest, are gifted with ease at martial or magical arts, but are specially gifted in the pursuit of akashic power. Most deva-asura belong to one of the akashic classes or have dabbled in them and even the few ones that don’t, develop modest akashic abilities. They also gravitate to the divine classes. If there is something peculiar about the deva-asura is that they never have levels in NPC classes, always developing unusual abilities even without training.

Names: Deva-asura commonly receive a name from their mortal parent, but they also have a special name that they innately know. Sharing it depends on the individual. The name is normally in the language most commonly associated with the divinity they were spawned from.

Both genders: Vināśa (annihilation), Taifū (typhoon), Xochitl (flower).

name: Drowmedary
racial_traits:
• +2 Constitution, +2 Charisma, –2 Dexterity: Drowmedary are tough and gregarious, but their twisted forms sometimes limit their mobility.

• Medium: Drowmedaries are medium creatures and thus have no bonuses or penalties for size.

• Humanoid (akashic, elf): Drowmedaries are humanoids with the akashic and elf subtypes.

• Normal Speed: Drowmedaries have a base speed of 30 feet.

• Bonus Essence: Drowmedaries have inherited the strong natural connection to the powers of Akasha from their gamla forebears, and start play with one bonus essence point in their pool.

• Poison Spit: Once per minute, a drowmedary can spit sedative poison at a creature within 10 feet as a standard action. The target must succeed at a Fortitude save (DC 10 + 1/2 the drowmedary's character level + the drowmedary's Constitution modifier) or become staggered for one round. If the target fails its save by 5 or more they instead fall asleep for one round. A drowmedary can invest essence into this ability as though it was a veil; for each point invested, its range increases by 5 feet and the save DC increases by 1.

• Tribal Teamwork: A drowmedary gains a teamwork feat he qualifies for as a bonus feat. As a move action, he may share this feat with all allied drowmedary within 60 feet for a number of rounds equal to his Charisma modifier (minimum 1).

• Languages: Dromedary begin play speaking Common and Undercommon. Drowmedary with high Intelligence scores can choose from the following bonus languages: Dwarf, Elf, Giant, Gnome, and Terran.

Akashic Racial Traits

All akashic creatures have the following racial traits unless noted otherwise:

• Essence Pool equal to racial hit die.

• Immunity to sleep effects.

• Low-light vision.

• Unless otherwise noted, akashic creatures always use Charisma as their primary veilweaving modifier. Akashic creatures with class levels use the primary veilweaving modifier of their class.

Alternate Racial Traits

Web Spit: Some drowmedaries lack the poison glands of their brethren, instead being infused with a spider-like essence. Once per minute, a drowmedary with this trait can spit web at a creature within 10 feet as a standard action. The target must succeed at a Reflex save (DC 10 + 1/2 the drowmedary's character level + the drowmedary's Constitution modifier) or be entangled for one round. Regardless of the result of this save, the target's space becomes difficult terrain for one round as well. A drowmedary can invest essence into this ability as though it was a veil; for each point invested, the range increases by 5 feet and the save DC increases by 1. This ability replaces poison spit.

Spidery Legs: Some drowmedaries have thinner limbs bristling with short hairs which grip like a spider's. They gain a climb speed of 20 feet. This ability replaces tribal teamwork.

Favored Class Bonuses

Drowmedary adventurers may use any alternate favored class bonus available to Drow or Gamla.
