description:
Deep in the caves beneath the desert sands, foul goddesses sought to fuse the essence of a drow with that of the wandering gamla. The end goal was to find a suitable punishment for those of their followers who thought that the form of a spider monster would be a nice change of pace from their normal backstabbing ways. What they got instead was the drowmedary, nomads of the caves below.

Appearance: Much smaller than the gamla, drowmedaries stand seven feet tall on average and have pitch-dark skin. The sparse fur which covers their bodies tends to be either deepest black or stark white, leaving only the palms of their hands and the surface of their faces exposed. They stand hunched, with a hump on their back. They are otherwise elflike, with long ears and finely chiseled features.

Demeanor: The Drowmedary are naturally distrustful of divine spellcasters and drow (much less drow divine spellcasters or those who claim a drow goddess as their patron), and are typically reclusive. Other than this, they tend to be generally friendly to those who help them or their families. As they wander, they trade with those they find. Others often find their lack of defined leadership puzzling, but this rarely causes long-term issues except with the most rigid of societies.

Background: Drowmedary started off few in number, but are incredibly long-lived. Although they have no natural predators, the deep caverns contain plenty of unnatural ones to keep their numbers down, and so their population grows slowly. They travel in bands organized roughly along family lines, but these nomadic bands tend to split once they get past 15-20 members. They are highly communal in nature, reacting almost as one to perceived threats down to the smallest child and considering the opinions of each member of the band in any major decision. Anyone can become anything they feel the call to be, and are encouraged to follow their natural inclinations regardless of any other factor. Drowmedary bands only rarely have designated overall leaders for this reason, they instead follow the one whose natural talents are best suited to the situation they currently face.

Understandably, given their origins, drowmedaries tend to shy away from true worship of deities, but hold concepts of community, hospitality, and open confrontation to be sacred. They tend to be strongly lawful and usually good alignments, but every individual is their own. Even the most evil among them, though, tend to be more ruthless and greedy than actively destructive.

Adventures: Drowmedaries tend to adventure for one of two reasons. The first and by far the most common is for the good of their band, with the intent to return after achieving something that needs to be done. The second is among those who feel that the society is too stifling, and run away in their own act of rebellion.

name: Fable Beast
racial_traits:
• Type: Fable beasts are magical beasts, with the akashic and augmented [animal] subtypes, but lack the darkvision trait the magical beast type normally grants.

• Animal Soul: Despite their expanded intellects and mystical abilities, fable beasts are still animals in many respects. For the purposes of spells and effects targeting creatures by type (such as a ranger’s favored enemy and bane weapons), fable beasts count as both magical beasts and animals. Fable beasts can't learn tricks or be targeted by awaken.

• Low-Light Vision: Fable beasts can see twice as far as humans in conditions of dim light.

• Scent (Ex): Fable beats have a heightened sense of smell. They have the scent special quality.

• Akashic Resistances: Fable beasts are akashic creatures, granting them immunity to magical sleep effects.

• Natural Armor: Fable beats gain a +1 natural armor bonus to their AC.

• Timeless: While they mature at a similar rate to normal animals of their type, fable beasts cease to age when they reach adulthood. However, they do have a maximum lifespan of 725 years. Additionally, they are not affected by spells and effects that would increase or decrease their age.

• Non-humanoid Anatomy: A fable beast is anatomically identical to a normal creature of their type. In most cases this means that they are quadrupedal, but this can vary. If a fable beast possesses 4 or more legs they gain +4 racial bonus to CMD against trip attempts.
• However, a fable beast is limited in the type of magic items and equipment they can use. They can wear magic items as normal for a creature of their body shape (see Table: Fable Beasts Magic Item Slots). However, fable beasts can wear and benefit from two magic rings regardless of their body shape. Their slotted magical items alter in size and shape to comfortably fit them when equipped. Fable beasts can shape and bind veils to any chakra normally, even if they do not possess the corresponding magic item slot. They must still meet any other prerequisites required to use a veil.

• Since they do not have hands, fable beasts cannot normally use, or easily manipulate, items that require the use of two hands or fine manual dexterity (such as wearing a shield, wielding a sword, or using a lock-pick). They can use such items that could reasonably be used or held with one hand and course motor control (e.g. door latches and wands), manipulating them with some combination of their mouths, paws, limbs, body, and other extremities but they take a -4 penalty if a Dexterity or Strength based check is required. However, they can not use their racial natural attacks while doing so.

• Heritage: All fable beasts must select a heritage that defines what type of animal they are. The available heritages are listed Here.

• Languages: Fable beasts begin play speaking Common and Fable. Fable beasts with high Intelligence scores can choose from the following: Dwarf, Elf, Giant, Gnome, Goblin, Halfling, Orc, Undercommon, and Sylvan.

Sidebar: Fable Language

The racial language “Fable” is a mixture of body language and various vocalizations that are largely indistinguishable from those of a normal creature of the fable beast's heritage, but can be used to transmit complex information. Speakers of this language can communicate freely with other fable beasts regardless of heritage, and can also exchange simple ideas and concepts with unintelligent creatures of their general type as if by speak with animals (e.g. a fable beast dog or wolf can communicate with normal wolves, dogs, coyotes, and jackals) and gain a +1 competence bonus on Handle Animal and Wild Empathy checks against normal animals that match their that can understand them via their use of Fable. This language has no written form. Like Druidic, Fable is a secret language. Fable beasts are born with an innate understanding of this language, and are not capable of teaching it to other creatures.

Akashic Racial Traits

All akashic creatures have the following racial traits unless noted otherwise:

• Essence Pool equal to racial hit die.

• Immunity to sleep effects.

• Low-light vision.

• Unless otherwise noted, akashic creatures always use Charisma as their primary veilweaving modifier. Akashic creatures with class levels use the primary veilweaving modifier of their class.

Alternate Racial Traits

• Adopted: Through random chance or misfortune some fable beasts find themselves being raised by well meaning humanoids that mistook them for normal creatures. A fable beast with this racial trait gains a +1 racial bonus on Bluff, Diplomacy, and Sense Motive checks when dealing with a one humanoid race of their choice reflecting their adopted family, and one of these skills is always a class skill for them. If their selected race has a racial language, that is not a secret language, they gain the ability to speak and understand it. This racial trait replaces natural armor.

• Apocalypse Beast: Some fable beasts are tied to tales of divine wrath. They must select a constellation from the Four Horsemen lesser cosmology or The Purple Rider lost constellation. They gain the ability to manifest all of the forms of the selected constellation. If they mark themselves with the selected constellation’s sign form, they can also have one other different manifestation of that constellation manifested at that same time. However, if the sign is dismissed so too is the other manifestation.

If they gain the constellations class feature, they treat constellations from the Four Horsemen lesser cosmology as lost constellations for the purposes of adding them to their cosmology and their selected constellation always replaces a constellation of the appropriate element in their cosmology.

Additionally they gain 1 point of essence that is deeply entwined with the energies of god-wrought ruin. !his essence can only be invested in or used to pay the essence cost for constellations from the Four Horsemen lesser cosmology or The Purple Rider lost constellation. This replaces their racial veil and inherent skills.

• Less Preternatural: The fable beast gains Skill Focus in one of their inherent skills and gains 1 point of bonus essence. This replaces their racial veil.

• Mystical Gifts: On rare occasions fable beasts are born with an affinity for magic that is uncommon in most of their kind, and are free of some of the limitations of their form with regard to its practice. They gain Eschew Materials as a bonus feat at 1st level and can perform the somatic components of spells with gestures appropriate for their form. However, any racial natural attacks they possess are treated as if they were one size category smaller. This alters a fable beast’s natural attacks.

• Tactile Telekinesis (Su): While touching an object in their possession a fable beast can manipulate it telekinetically as if they were a humanoid creature using one hand. The object can not require more than a number of pounds equal to their Charisma score (+5 pounds for each size category they are above medium size; -5 pounds for each size category they are below medium size) of force to move or manipulate. They can always "let go" of an object as a free action. With this ability, a lever can be pulled, a key can be turned, a rope tied, a root chopped, barding donned or doffed, a lock can be picked, and so on as long as the force required is within the weight limitation of this ability. The weight of objects being carried by a fable beast via this ability does count towards their carry capacity. This ability can be invested with essence. For each point of essence invested in this ability the fable beast increases the amount of weight they can manipulate using it by 1 pound. This replaces the fable beast's racial veil.

Sidebar: Fable Beasts and Apocalyptic Signs

The Four Horsemen lesser cosmology and The Purple Rider lost constellation all have signs that are intended to benefit creatures of the animal type. Fable beasts, due to their animal soul ability, are eligible targets for such signs. However, when marked by one of these signs the effects of it on a fable beast are altered as follows:
• A fable beast is always considered to be their own master.

• In place of a sacred bonus to Ride, they instead gain a sacred bonus to Acrobatics equal to the marking zodiac’s Charisma modifier.

• They never gain the benefits of the sign’s enlarge person effect. Nor do they gain temporary hit points or a sacred bonuses to AC, saving throws, and melee attack or damage rolls from the sign

• The sign does not grant them bonus tricks.
