description:
Animals are common figures in fables, fairy tales, and stories of myriad description. Quite often despite often being otherwise no different from their real world counterparts, they are sapient and have the power of speech. In such tales wolves are sly, dogs are stalwart guardians, and hares are swift though foolish.

Physical Description: Fable beasts are physically indistinguishable from normal creatures of their type. However, they tend to appear to be exceptional examples of their animal type. Their sizes, coloration, and other physical traits can be representative of any subspecies or breed of their relative type, but in general they represent the most common variety in their location of birth.

Society: Fable beasts tend to live in a manner that would be considered normal for a creature of their general description. In some cases living with normal animals of their kind. However, it is nor unheard of for fable beasts to integrate in various ways with humanoid societies.

Relations: Most fable beasts tend to be aloof with members of other races, and with fable beasts of different heritages, but there are common exceptions. For example fable beats of the dog heritage tend to get along well with most humanoids.

Alignment and Religion: Fable beasts tend towards neutrality, as extremes of morality or ethics are not a natural part of their thinking. While not known for their affinity for religion, they do hold a certain innate veneration for nature and it is not unheard of for fable beasts to follow gods of nature. The nature deity most commonly followed by religious fable beasts is the akashic deity Progenitor.

Adventurers: Fable beasts adventurers are fairly uncommon with most heritages. Fable beasts most often become adventurers in response to circumstances that threaten their homes or their lesser kin. Fable beasts in general have a predisposition towards classes with a connection to nature such as huay and rangers, but classes that are in keeping with their natural abilities, lore, or natures are also common (e.g. eclipse ravens and lion fisherkings).

Names: Fable beast names are often reflective of some physical or behavioral trait they possess. A fable beast known for howling might be called Howl, and another with bright red fur may be called Red. The normal exception would be fable beasts that were raised by humanoids. Such fable beasts often have names that fit their adopted culture.

name: Gamla
racial_traits:
• +2 Constitution, +2 Wisdom, –2 Dexterity: Gamla are tough and hardy, with calm and focused natures, but their large and ungainly bodies are somewhat slow and clumsy.

• Essence-Born: Gamla are humanoids with the akashic subtype.

• Large: Gamla are Large and take a –1 size penalty to AC and attack rolls, a –4 size penalty on stealth checks, and gain a +1 size bonus to their CMB and CMD. In addition, a gamla's large size gives them a natural reach of 10 ft.

• Speed: Gamla have a base land speed of 30 feet.

• Undersized Weapons: The gamla have crude ungulate hands which make it difficult for them to wield properly sized weapons. As such, gamla's ability to wield manufactured weapons is determined as though the gamla were one size category smaller than they actually are.

• Desert Strider: A gamla moves through nonmagical difficult terrain in desert environments at normal speed.

• Endurance: Gamla's ability to store fatty, moisture rich deposits in their bodies allows them to sustain themselves through harsh and extended conditions. Gamla gain Endurance as a bonus feat.

• Bonus Essence: Gamla have a natural connection to the unique power of akasha and start play with 1 bonus essence point in their pool.

• Spit (Ex): Once per minute a gamla can regurgitate some of the contents of its stomach, spitting the disgusting phlegm that results at a single enemy within 10 feet as a swift action. The target must succeed at a Fortitude save (DC 10 + 1/2 level + Con modifier) or be sickened for 1d4 rounds. The gamla can invest essence into this ability as though it were a veil; for each point of essence invested, the save DC of the spit attack increases by 1 and the duration is extended by 1 round.

• Languages: Gamla begin play speaking Common and Sylvan. Gamla with high Intelligence scores can choose from the following: Elven, Giant, Gnome, and Terran.

Akashic Racial Traits

All akashic creatures have the following racial traits unless noted otherwise:

• Essence Pool equal to racial hit die.

• Immunity to sleep effects.

• Low-light vision.

• Unless otherwise noted, akashic creatures always use Charisma as their primary veilweaving modifier. Akashic creatures with class levels use the primary veilweaving modifier of their class.

Favored Class Options

The following favored class options are available to all characters of this race who have the listed favored class, and unless otherwise stated, the bonus applies each time you select the favored class reward.

• Barbarian: Increase fast movement bonus by 1 foot (only effective in increments of 5). ([[]])

• Bard: Increase bonus to Knowledge skills from bardic knowledge by +1/4. ([[]])

• Cleric: Gain +1/4 use of channel energy. ([[]])

• Daevic: Gain +1/5 point of essence. ([[]])

• Druid: Add +1/2 to wild empathy checks made to influence creatures from desert environments. ([[]])

• Fighter: Gain +1/4 bonus to CMB rolls against targets affected by your spit ability. ([[]])

• Guru: (Akasin) Add +1/4 to CMB rolls made with weapons from the polearm fighter weapon training group. ([[]])

• Helmsman: Bonded vessel's reach increases by +1 ft. (Arcforge: Technology Expanded)

• Promethean: Treat one veil from the Shapes of the Primordial set as having +1/6 additional essence invested; this does not count against your normal limit on invested essence. ([[]])

• Ranger: Increase favored terrain bonuses by +1/4 (Desert or Plains only). ([[]])

• Rogue: Increase trapfinding bonus by +1/2 in pyramids and desert environments. ([[]])

• Sorcerer: Add one spell known from the sorcerer spell list. This spell must have the Fire descriptor and be at least one level below the highest spell level the sorcerer can cast. ([[]])

• Truenamer: Learn +1/5 of a bonus recitation. The recitation must be at least one level below the highest level recitation the truenamer can recite, and its similar school must have the fire descriptor. (Akashic Tales: Relics of Atlantis)

• Vizier: Gain +1/5 point of essence. ([[]])

• Warder: Add +1/4 bonus to attack rolls made with shields. ([[]])

• Wizard: Increase caster level for [Fire] spells by +1/3. ([[]])

Alternate Gamla

Alqarn (Rhinofolk)

Alqarn are fiercely strong and quick to anger, valuing family and friendship above all else. An alqarn who believes a loved one is threatened will fight to the death without a second thought.

• Ability Modifiers: +2 Str, +2 Con, –2 Wis

• Slow and Steady: Alqarn have a base speed of 20 feet, but their speed is never modified by armor or encumbrance.

• Ferocity: Once per day, when an alqarn is brought below 0 hit points but not killed, they can fight on for 1 more round as if disabled. At the end of their next turn, unless brought to above 0 hit points, they immediately fall unconscious and begins dying. This replaces the endurance racial trait.

• Gore: Alqarn are known for the mighty horn that grows from their snout. This horn grants them a gore natural attack dealing 1d8 damage. The alqarn can invest essence into this ability as though it were a veil; the alqarn's gore gains an enhancement bonus equal to the amount of essence invested. If at least three points of essence are invested in this ability, the alqarn sprouts a secondary horn just behind the first that enhances their destructive capacity, increasing the base damage of their gore to 2d6. This replaces the desert strider and spit racial traits.

Feelkha (Elephantfolk)

Feelkha are stern and slow-tempered being who value study and reason above all else. Their great size and hardy constitution is often perceived as being strangely at odds with their studious nature.

• Ability Modifiers: +2 Con, +2 Int, –2 Dex

• Slow and Steady: Feelkha have a base speed of 20 feet, but their speed is never modified by armor or encumbrance.

• Trunk: Feelkha have long, flexible proboscises that can be used to carry items. While they cannot wield weapons with these trunks, they can use them to retrieve small, stowed objects carried on their persons as a swift action. The feelkha can invest essence into this ability as though it were a veil; when at least one point of essence is invested in their trunk, they gain a slam natural attack dealing 1d6 damage. This trunk is treated as a magical weapon for the purposes of overcoming damage reduction. For each additional point of essence invested, the feelkha's trunk gains a new special ability in the following order: reach, disarm, grab, constrict. Once all of these abilities have been unlocked, any further essence invested increases the damage dealt by the trunk by one size category per point of essence. This replaces the desert strider and spit racial traits.
description:
The gamla are a race of bipedal humanoids who appear to be a cross between a half-giant and a camel. These sturdy nomads travel in small communities throughout the deserts they call home, always seeking new sources of water and shelter.

Physical Description: Gamla tower over 8 feet tall, with long, gangly arms and legs and thick torsos. Their backs appear somewhat lumpy and misshapen due to the fatty stores of moisture and nutrients they carry in the humps on their backs.

Society: Gamla are nomadic herdsman and traders, always seeking the next horizon. They typically travel in groups consisting of 4 to 5 extended families, each with their own specialty they bring to the tribe. Oftentimes it is the responsibility of younger male gamla to leave the tribe and seek out a mate to bring a fresh influx of blood and skills into the community.

Relations: Gamla are a calm and good-natured people and get along well with most other races, though the frantic pace of many human settlements is sometimes unsettling to the gamla, who tend to move at the pace nature dictates.

Alignment and Religion: Gamla tend towards neutral alignments, as their slower-paced upbringing often teaches them to approach everything with a calm and measured mindset.

Adventurers: Many young gamla who leave their tribes in search of a mate or skillset stumble upon the adventuring life almost by accident. Their large size makes them favored caravan guards, both for their ability to see above crowds and for the intimidation factor provided by their bulk. Others naturally gravitate towards established adventuring groups since the close-knit nature and generally open minds of such people tend to make the gamla feel at home.

name: Hanabito
racial_traits:
Standard Racial Traits

• +2 Cha, +2 Con, –4 Str, –2 Wis: Hanabito have a gentle innocence that charms those around them, and due to their origins they are oddly hearty. However, they are vulnerable to deceptions and their light builds predispose them to being physically weak.

• Size: Hanabito are Medium creatures and thus receive no bonuses or penalties due to their size.

• Type: Hanabito are Constructs with the akashic, augmented, plant, and living machine subtypes.

• Normal Speed: A hanabito has a base speed of 30 feet.

• Languages: Hanabito begin play speaking Common. Hanabito with high Intelligence scores can choose from the following: Dwarf, Elf, Giant, Gnome, Goblin, Halfling, Orc, Undercommon Sylvan.

Defense Racial Traits

• Akashic Immunity: As akashic creatures, hanabito are immune to magical sleep effects.

Magical Racial Traits

• Bonus Essence: Hanabito have a natural connection to the unique power of akasha and start play with 1 bonus point of essence in their pool.

• Echoes of Paradise: Hanabito have the ability to resonate their voices with the planar frequency of their paradisiacal mother plane. They gain the ability to shape the Songstress's Voice veil. This veil does not count against their normal limits on veils shaped, or their veil slots. They use their highest mental ability score's modifier as their veilweaving modifier for this veil, and their character level as their veilweaver level. If they gain the ability to shape this veil from another source, they increase its DC by +1.

• Planar Tuning Fork (Su): The flowers that hanabito are created from are native to other planes of existence. These planes are generally heavenly planes, fey realms, or paradise-like demiplanes. Due to this, members of this race are drawn to a single plane reflecting the plane that spawned their botanical heritage. This planar origin is selected at character creation and can never be changed. They are aware of the identity of their "mother" plane and gain a +1 racial bonus on all Knowledge (planes) related to this plane and its denizens.

A hanabito, or any creature that is touching them, can use them as a focus for planeshift and similar effects in place of “forked metal rod attuned to the plane of travel” that such effects normally require. Additionally, they are automatically aware of if a portal they can see leads to their mother plane, and once per week they can cast locate portal as a spell-like ability. The effects of this spell-like ability only aid in the locating of portals leading to the hanabito's mother plane.

• Plant Sympathy (Su): Hanabito have an affinity for other plants, and their herbaceous kin will aid them in small ways. Hanabito have the ability to converse with plants as if subject to a continual speak with plants effect. Essence can be bound to this ability. As long as at least 1 point of essence is bound to this ability. Hanabito can move through natural difficult terrain at their normal speed as long as the difficult terrain results from living plants and foliage. Magically altered terrain affects them normally. For each point of essence bound after the first, they gain a 5 ft. enhancement bonus to their base land speed in situations where this ability would apply.-

Offense Racial Traits

• Poison Blood: What flows in the veins of a hanabito is poisonous to other creatures. By taking a point of essence drain, a hanabito can concentrate the alchemical toxins in their blood as follows:

To envenom a weapon that they wield or natural weapon they possess as a swift action: This option requires the hanabito to be injured when they use this ability. A hanabito is immune to the effects of their own poison. When used in this way, their blood functions as an injury poison.

In response to being hit by a natural attack or unarmed strike as an immediate action: The attacking creature is exposed to the hanabito's toxic blood, which functions as a contact poison.
Hanabito Blood: contact or injury (see text); Save Fort DC 10 + 1/2 the hanabito's Hit Dice + the hanabito's Constitution modifier; Frequency 1/round for 4 rounds; Effect 1 Con; Onset 1 round; Cure 1 save.

Temporary essence can't be used to activate this ability.

Other Racial Traits

• Florunculus: Hanabito were all originally extraplanar flowers that were converted into humanoid shapes via akashically catalyzed alchemical processes similar to those used to craft constructs. Due to this process, they lack a construct's normal immunity to mind-affecting effects (charms, compulsions, morale effects, patterns, and phantasms), bleed, disease, death effects, necromancy effects, paralysis, poison, and stunning. They must also breathe, hydrate, and consume nutrients except for as described in their photosynthesis racial ability.

For the purposes of effects targeting creatures by type (such as a ranger's favored enemy and bane weapons), hanabito count both as constructs and as plants. A hanabito gains a +1 racial bonus on saving throws against mind-affecting effects, disease, paralysis, and poison. This trait can be invested with essence. For each point of essence invested, this racial bonus increases by +1.

• Photosynthesis (Su): Hanabito, have the ability to persist on light and water. As long as a hanabito can consume twice the normal amount of water required by a creature of their size and is exposed to at least 1 hour of sun or moon light, they do not need to eat for 24 hours.

Senses Racial Traits

• Darkvision: Hanabito have darkvision and so can see perfectly in the dark up to 60 feet.

• Low-Light Vision: Hanabito can see twice as far as humans in conditions of dim light.

Skill Racial Traits

• Beast Lure: Hanabito gain a +1 alchemical bonus on Handle Animal and wild empathy checks made to influence creatures with the animal type; this also applies to Diplomacy checks against fable beasts. A creature must be able to smell a hanabito's scent for the hanabito to benefit from this ability.

This ability can be invested with essence and is treated as having the aroma descriptor for the purposes of investment with aspected essence. For each point of essence invested in this ability, the bonus it grants increases by +1.

Weakness Racial Traits

• Conspicuous Flower: Due to their strong pleasant odor and bright hair coloration, hanabito suffer -2 on Stealth checks related to hiding their presence.

• Fire Vulnerability: Hanabito have vulnerability to fire.

Alternate Racial Traits

Aromatic Essence (Su): Some hanabito have nature's tightly tied to their natural floral aroma. They gain 2 points of aroma-aspected essence. This essence can only be invested in a veil or essence receptacle that has the aroma descriptor. This replaces bonus essence.

Fertile Soil: Hanabito with levels in the sorcerer class that have the Verdant bloodline, treat their Charisma score as 2 points higher for all sorcerer spells and class abilities. Clerics who are members of this race with the Plant domain use their domain powers and spells at +1 caster level. This trait does not give members of this race early access to level-based powers; it only affects powers that they could already use without this trait. This trait replaces plant sympathy.

Honorary “Pack” Member: Some hanabito spend their time almost exclusively with members of a specific fable beast heritage of their choice. These hanabito gain the ability to understand, but not speak, Fable as if they were a fable beast of their chosen heritage. They also gain a +2 racial bonus on Handle Animal and wild empathy checks made against animals of the same general type (e.g. canines or felines) as their chosen fable beast heritage They also gain this bonus to Diplomacy and Sense Motive checks against fable beasts of their chosen heritage. This trait replaces beast lure.

Inherent Alchemy (Su): Some hanabito are especially attuned to the akashic and alchemical processes of their body. They can tap these processes to wield alchemical power against others. They gain the ability to shape Aldain's Alchemical Armory. This veil does not count against their normal limits on veils shaped, or their veil slots. They use their highest mental ability score's modifier as their veilweaving modifier for this veil, and their character level as their veilweaver level. This replaces echoes of paradise.

Living Anodyne (Su): Some hanabito literally bring a taste of paradise to those around them. They gain the ability to shape Floral Nostrum. This veil does not count against their normal limits on veils shaped, or their veil slots. They use their highest mental ability score's modifier as their veilweaving modifier for this veil, and their character level as their veilweaver level. This replaces echoes of paradise.

Petal Child: These hanabito are supernaturally light and the wind can aid movements like a sakura petal or a dandelion seed. They gain the ability to shape Petalstep Soles. This veil does not count against their normal limits on veils shaped, or their veil slots. They use their highest mental ability score's modifier as their veilweaving modifier for this veil, and their character level as their veilweaver level. This replaces echoes of paradise.

Song of Rest: Many hanabito are famous for their musical abilities, and some of them channel this talent into an ability to help other creatures rest and recover. Hanabito with this trait gain a +1 racial bonus to Perform (sing), and can use restful sleep once per day as a spell-like ability. The caster level for this ability equals the hanabito's number of hit dice. This trait replaces poison blood
description:
The promise of an idealized, unspoiled, paradise has an almost universal appeal for sapient creatures. Some try to reach such a place through diligent search for secret places and portals, but others seek to manufacture tools to aid in their task. The hanabito race is one such tool. This race was crafted by using advanced alchemy and akashic magic to transform rare flowers, which were believed to come from paradise-like realms, into human form. This was done in the hopes that the resultant creatures would act as living guides, instinctively drawn to the place of their origin, or else function as sympathetic links to such realms.

The means to produce hanabito have mostly been lost to the ages. “New” hanabito are usually only discovered in the depths of ancient laboratories inside of sealed tanks of potent alchemical preservatives, or under the effects of magical stasis effects. It is also exceptionally rare to encounter infant or child members of this artificial race.

Physical Description: Hanabito look like humans with beautiful features and slender frames. They are also relatively light weight for a creature of their size. However, it is their bright floral colored hair, and their red eyes that visually sets them apart. Aside from this, hanabito always emit a pleasant aroma of fresh flowers that is especially enticing to animals.

Society: Being an artificially created race with limited numbers, hanabito do not have a society of their own. The only cultural commonality is that most wear remnants from their creation processes, such as metallic collars or manacles. However, hanabito do integrate easily with societies that venerate and respect the natural world.

Relations: Their subtly alien demeanor can be slightly off putting to most other races that they encounter, but their unearthly beauty often counters this. They get along well with most races, and their almost childlike naivety and innocence has a tendency to endear them to others. Hanabito are known to be especially fond of fable beasts, enjoying the enthusiastic attention that is often directed at them by these sapient animals.

Alignment and Religion: Hanabito are not naturally prone to many of the vices that plague other races such as extremism, greed, hatred, and bigotry. While they could come to understand these concepts, a hanabito is unlikely to internalize them. They are usually neutral good, and gravitate towards the worship of deities of nature, beauty, and music.

Adventurers: Hanabito are not naturally predisposed to seeking out adventure. However, they will happily travel with adventurous friends and companions, often gravitating towards support and healing roles. Classes such as bard, oracle, and radiant are common choices among hanabito.

Names: Hanabito names are normally consistent with those of the society they live in, or that of their creator.

name: Living Veil
racial_traits:
• Ability Score Modifier: Living veils are naturally tied to the powers of akasha, and this attunement causes their minds to be attuned with their veilweaving as well. They gain +2 to one of the following ability scores: Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma.

• Size: Living veils are Medium creatures and thus receive no bonuses or penalties due to their size.

• Type: Living veils are Constructs with the akashic and living veil subtypes.

• Base Speed: Living veils have a base speed of 30 feet.

• Languages: Living veils begin play speaking Common. Due to their innate connection to the Akashic Record, living veils with high Intelligence scores can choose any language as a bonus language (except secret languages, such as Druidic).

Senses Racial Traits

• Darkvision: Living veils have darkvision 60 ft.

• Low-Light Vision: Living veils can see twice as far as humans in conditions of dim light.

Magic Racial Traits

• Living Veil: The living veil is a veil given life, which forms their core which their body builds its makeup from. The living veil selects a single veil of any slot (including esoteric chakra, such as Blood, Interface, Ring, Storm, or Voice), to act as their base veil, which determines their appearance and abilities. They are always treated as having this veil shaped, and it does not count against their veils shaped or their veil slots. They use their highest mental ability score as their veilweaving modifier, their character level as their veilweaving level with this veil, and they always treat this veil as if it were invested with its maximum possible allotment of essence. If their base veil can be shaped in multiple chakra slots, they choose a single slot to consider it shaped in, and can change this slot by spending one hour in meditation. If the living veil chooses a veil shaped in an esoteric chakra, this ability does not grant the living veil to normally shape veils in that slot. This veil cannot be disguised and cannot be sundered. If this veil would be suppressed (such as if the living veil chooses to suppress it, if it is dispelled, or if the living veil enters an anti-magic field), the living veil is staggered for the duration of the suppression. This veil cannot be permanently suppressed or unshaped. If their veil would be affected by such an effect, it is instead only suppressed temporarily for 1 hour. While the living veil’s base veil is suppressed the living veil’s appearance becomes dull and translucent.

• Essential Life: Living veils have a Constitution score and can be targeted by spells and effects that target living creatures or constructs, as well as effects that require Fortitude saving throws. They do not gain an immunity to ability damage, ability drain, energy drain, exhaustion, or fatigue. They do not gain Size Bonus Hit Points from their construct typing. Living veils are not destroyed when they reach 0 hit points. A living veil reduced to -1 or fewer hit points is knocked unconscious and automatically stabilizes. Their physical form becomes nearly transparent while unconscious due to the suppression of their base veil. A living veil that has an amount of negative hit points equal to its Constitution score is destroyed, leaving behind a mass of faintly glowing ash and dust as its corpse. This ash and dust begins to decay at the same rate as the corpse of a living creature, and can be affected by spells or effects that would slow its decay.

• Sentient Magic: Living veils do not gain the normal immunity to mind-affecting effects possessed by a Construct, and possess a soul which allows them to be raised and resurrected as if they were a living creature, but they cannot be affected by the reincarnate spell.

• Malleable Form: Living veils have a great deal of control over their physical form, however it will always be affected by their base veil which affects the color, textures, and shapes of their form. As a standard action they can change their appearance and the structure of their body, however their appearance must always resemble a bipedal Medium-sized Humanoid creature with two arms, and a head. The living veil otherwise has full cosmetic control over their form, allowing them to mimic the forms of other Humanoids or to take on a more alien or unique appearance. These cosmetic changes provide no mechanical benefits or penalties to the living veil (if they choose to have wings, they don’t provide flight, for example).

Alternate Racial Traits

• Aberrant Essence: The living veil’s base veil is particularly aberrant, giving them traits more like a living creature than a construct. The living veil must choose a veil with the [Fleshwarp] descriptor as their base veil. The living veil is an aberration rather than a construct, and gains a +2 bonus to their Constitution score. A living veil with this trait does not need to eat, drink, breathe, or sleep to survive. At 1st level, the living veil can choose to use their Constitution to determine their veilweaving modifier for their base veil. They only use their Constitution to determine their veilweaving modifier for their base veil if they choose to do so, not any other veils they can shape. When the living veil dies, their body collapses into a mass of flesh, viscera, and bone rather than a pile of glowing ash. This body can be returned to life as normal for a dead creature, and decays at the normal rates for one as well. This racial trait modifies the living veil’s living veil trait, and replaces their essential life and sentient magic traits.

• Aquatic Form: The living veil was created or appeared underwater, and their base form adjusted to suit their environment. The living veil has a swim speed of 20 feet and gains a +8 racial bonus to Swim checks. Living veils with this trait have a base speed of 20 feet. This trait replaces the living veil’s base speed.

• Compact Form: A living veil with this trait has a more compact form than other living veils. They are Small creatures, gaining a +1 size bonus to their AC, a +1 size bonus on attack rolls, a -1 penalty on CMB and CMD, and a +4 size bonus on Stealth checks. Living veils with this trait have a base speed of 20 feet. This racial trait replaces the living veil's size and base speed.

• Constructed Essence: The living veil is sentient, but their form is a magical construct that was created rather than essence given shape. Living veils with this trait lose their essential life trait. They do not have a Constitution score and follow the normal rules for constructs (gaining Size Bonus Hit Points, being destroyed at 0 hit points, etc). The form of a destroyed living veil with this trait varies for each living veil, but no matter the form their destroyed body takes it cannot be raised from the dead or resurrected. They can still be affected by effects that restore a destroyed construct, as normal. This racial trait replaces the living veil’s essential construction trait and modifies their sentient magic trait.

• Incarnate Form: Rather than having a physical form, the living veil must attach themselves to other willing creatures. The living veil gains the effects of gaseous form as a constant extraordinary effect. The living veil cannot end this effect through normal means, and it cannot be dispelled or otherwise suppressed except by being affected by a polymorph effect or using their incarnate form ability (see below). The living veil can speak while in their gaseous form, can enter liquids (treating their fly speed as their swim speed), and can reinvest their essence, but they cannot use and do not benefit from the effects of their veils while in their gaseous form. The living veil cannot pick up or interact normally with items and objects while in their gaseous form, but they can incorporate an unattended slotted magic item into their essence by spending 1 hour in meditation while touching that item. An incorporated item is considered to be worn by the living veil, they can only incorporate one item per magic item slot, and they can choose to expel an incorporated magic item as a full round item. Any incorporated magic items are also expelled if the living veil is slain. If the living veil has the Aquatic form trait, they can enter water while in their gaseous form. Additionally, the living veil gains the incarnate form class feature of the Incarnate Veil class. If the living veil takes that class, they gain a bonus feat of their choice at 1st level. This replaces the living veil’s malleable form trait.

Akashic Racial Traits

All akashic creatures have the following racial traits unless noted otherwise:

• Essence Pool equal to racial hit die.

• Immunity to sleep effects.

• Low-light vision.

• Unless otherwise noted, akashic creatures always use Charisma as their primary veilweaving modifier. Akashic creatures with class levels use the primary veilweaving modifier of their class.
description:
These strange creatures are veils given life and sentience, forming bodies composed of pure essence which they can shape using their will with a single veil at its core which influences their minds and lifestyles. Scholars and mages argue whether these creatures are truly alive, or merely an echo of the Akashic Record acting upon the world, but living veils insist that they are truly living beings with their own sentience and life, not merely memories stored within the Record which have been cast out into the world.

Physical Description: Each living veil is a unique being; they do not possess standard appearances or personalities, often acting entirely alien to other living beings upon their creation. The process of their creation is also often unique to each living veil: some are formed accidentally from an abundance of naturally occurring essence, some from the echo of a powerful veilweavers soul upon their death, or are willfully created by a mad mage or insane vizier.

Living veils all possess a base veil that makes up their core which influences their appearance, beliefs, and abilities. A living veil formed from a weapon-producing veil may be more headstrong and have an appearance that resembles their weapon on a metaphorical level, or a living veil with a nature-aligned base veil having a serene personality and an appearance that resembles plants or vines. No manner their appearance or personality, living veils always appear quite unnatural, having bodies formed of translucent essence of a few colors drawn from their base veil. The living veil’s base veil is always a core part of their appearance, the rest of their body seeming to flow from it. When their base veil is invested with essence, the living veil’s form appears to become energized alongside it.

Living veils usually have a great deal of control over their body, allowing them to easily change their appearance, but always resembled bipedal humanoids. Living veils do not possess a sex, though some choose to identify as a single gender and modify their form to fit that gender’s norms in the society they choose to live in. This control over their forms allows them other conveniences, allowing them to change their body to appear clothed, tattooed, pierced, or possessing any other cosmetic features as their imagination desires.

Society: Living veils tend to seek out others of their kind, forming tight-knit groups that exist within broader society as a whole. Due to their extreme rarity, living veils rarely have a society that they truly call their own. Living veils whose base veil is non-combat focused tend to take up the skills and abilities granted by that veil and utilize it to specialize on a career within society, should that society be accepting of their unusual nature.

Relations: Living veils are given life with all of the faculties and skills of an adult creature without the life spent growing to that point and this leads to most of them being quite curious of mortal natures, attempting to join their communities and learn their ways.

Alignment and Religion: Living veils are usually of no particular alignment, and as a whole follow no specific gods. Living veils born into a community will commonly take up worship of local deities in an effort to emulate those living within said communities, but unless said living veil takes up the mantle of a divine class they rarely truly grasp the concept of religion due to their ageless natures.

Adventurers: Veils as a whole are tools of combat, which leads to many living veils who have such veils as their base veil to take up the mantle of an adventurer. With their innate combat abilities granted by their veil, and their curiosity to experience a world that is brand new to them, living veils commonly take up the call of adventure.
source: Races of Obscura - Araenae and Living Veils, pgs. 8–10

name: Sobek
racial_traits:
• +2 Strength, +2 Charisma, –2 Wisdom: Sobek are strong with forceful personalities, but their predatory instincts often lead them to making rash decisions.

• Essence-Born, Reptilian: Sobek are humanoids with the akashic and reptilian subtypes.

• Medium: Sobek are medium creatures and have no bonuses or penalties due to their size.

• Slow Land Speed: Sobek are poorly built for moving long distances across the ground and have a base speed of 20 feet.

• Water Adaptation: Sobek are natural swimmers, with strong tails that help propel them through the water, granting them a swim speed of 30 feet. In addition, a sobek can hold their breath for a number of rounds equal to 4 times their Constitution score before they risk drowning.

• Natural Camouflage: Whenever the sobek is in Swamp or Water terrains, he gains a +2 bonus to Stealth checks. A sobek can invest essence into this ability the same way they would invest it into a veil or feat making it even more effective; they gain an additional +2 to Stealth checks for each point of essence invested into this ability.

• Bite: The sobek gains a bite attack dealing 1d6 damage plus their Strength modifier. This bite can be enhanced by investing essence into it; the sobek gains an enhancement bonus to attack and damage with his bite attack equal to the amount of essence invested.

• Sweeping Tail: The sobek’s powerful tail makes them difficult to sneak up on. Whenever a creature would move into a flanking position, the sobek may make a tail slap attack against them as an attack of opportunity. This attack deals 1d6 points of damage plus 1/2 his Strength modifier, and if successful, the sobek may attempt a free trip maneuver against the opponent.

• Bonus Essence: Sobek have a natural connection to akashic energy and start play with 1 bonus essence point in their pool.

• Languages: Sobek begin play speaking Common. Sobek with high Intelligence scores can choose from the following: Aquan, Draconic, Dwarven, Goblin, Gnoll and Orc.

Akashic Racial Traits

All akashic creatures have the following racial traits unless noted otherwise:

• Essence Pool equal to racial hit die.

• Immunity to sleep effects.

• Low-light vision.

• Unless otherwise noted, akashic creatures always use Charisma as their primary veilweaving modifier. Akashic creatures with class levels use the primary veilweaving modifier of their class.

Favored Class Options

The following favored class options are available to all characters of this race who have the listed favored class, and unless otherwise stated, the bonus applies each time you select the favored class reward.

• Barbarian: Increase the essence capacity of bite or natural camouflage by +1/4. ([[]])

• Bard: Increase the competence bonus granted by your inspire courage performance by +1/5. ([[]])

• Cleric: Increase caster level for [Water] spells by +1/3. ([[]])

• Daevic: Treat one of your Passion veils as being invested with +1/5 additional essence. ([[]])

• Druid: Add +1/2 to wild empathy checks made to influence creatures from watery environments. ([[]])

• Fighter: Gain +1/4 bonus to CMB on trip attempts. ([[]])

• Guru: (Sineater) Add +1/2 to unarmed strike damage rolls. ([[]])

• Helmsman: Bonded vessel gains +1/3 natural armor bonus. (Arcforge: Technology Expanded)

• Paladin: Gain +1/6 of a new Mercy. ([[]])

• Promethean: Treat one veil from the Warsculpter’s Menagerie set as having +1/6 additional essence invest ed; this does not count against your normal limit on invested essence. ([[]])

• Rajah: Increase the damage done by natural attacks granted by your race by 1/4. ([[]])

• Ranger: Increase favored terrain bonuses by +1/4 (Swamp or Water only). ([[]])

• Rogue: Add +1/2 to sneak attack damage rolls made with bite attack. ([[]])

• Sorcerer: Add one spell known from the sorcerer spell list. This spell must have the Water descriptor and be at least one level below the highest spell level the sorcerer can cast. ([[]])

• Vizier: Gain +1/5 point of essence. ([[]])

• Wizard: Increase caster level for Water spells by +1/3. ([[]])
description:
The sobek are a race of reptilian humanoids who prefer to live near areas of running water. They are proud and fierce creatures, as at home in the rivers of their native lands as they are fighting in the plains or dunes. The sobek are a young race, and nearly all of them are born with some facility for manipulating the mysterious force known as akasha.

Physical Description: Sobek are stocky, with ridged and mottled skin and short, almost stumpy, limbs. Sobek range in coloration from sandy tan to murky green or gray, with their particular coloration usually matching the habitat they were born in. The average sobek stands just over 5 feet tall, though their powerful tails are nearly as long again as their body.

Society: Much of a male sobek’s life is spent engaging in struggles for dominance, both social and physical. The females are often just as fierce as the males, though typically they are more inclined to shame a rival through a clever social manipulation than a show of brute force. Despite, or perhaps because of, their highly competitive natures, the sobek are a highly social bunch, often living in large closely-knit communities. Sobek are fiercely protective of their young until they reach the age of maturity, at which point they are regarded as rivals just as dangerous as any other member of the race

Relations: The sobek are a young race that has only recently developed relationships with the outside world. They find that they have much in common with proud and war-like races such as orcs, though their strictly structured hierarchy is much more disciplined than those chaotic creatures. The sobek also appreciate trade with dwarven tribes, as the reliable and strong nature of that race appeals to them. Humans and elves they view as flighty and inconsistent; gnomes and halflings are viewed with similar disdain, often compounded by deep-seated instincts that tell the sobek that such small, soft creatures are surely prey.

Alignment and Religion: The sobek tend towards lawful alignments, always following the strict hierarchy established by their complex and instinctive dominance rituals. A sobek who would seek to overrule another must prove his superiority. The sobek have no concept of religion, except in a vague naturalistic philosophy they refer to as the river of existence. This philosophy believes that all energy flows through the various planes and levels of existence and the sobeks are travelers riding its current. When a sobek passes on, they believe that their spirit merely flows on to a new or greater tributary.

Adventurers: The sobek are incredibly fierce warriors and rarely have trouble finding employment as mercenaries. Young sobek who disagree with the elders of their communities but lack the strength to supplant them will often leave to acquire experience and strength before returning to take their rightful place as leader. Others will seek to apprentice themselves to particularly skilled members of other races, hoping to learn the martial or social skills necessary to rise to a position of strength and leadership.
source:
Akashic Mysteries, pgs. 33–35
Akashic Tales: Red's Peril, pg. 10

name: Suqur
racial_traits:
• +2 Dexterity, +2 Intelligence, –2 Constitution: Suqur are exceptionally agile and have keen minds, but their hollow-boned bodies are particularly fragile.

• Essence-Born: Suqur are humanoids with the akashic subtype.

• Medium: Suqur are medium creatures and have no bonuses or penalties due to their size.

• Speed: Suqur have a base land speed of 20 feet.

• Low-light Vision: Suqur can see twice as far as humans in conditions of dim light.

• Glide: The suqur’s wings allow them to glide. A suqur can make a DC 15 Fly check to fall safely from any height without taking falling damage, as if using feather fall. When falling safely, it may make an additional DC 15 Fly check to glide, moving 5 feet laterally for every 10 feet it falls. In addition, the suqur may invest essence into his wings to improve their capabilities. A suqur who invests at least one point of essence into his wings gains a fly speed of 15 feet, with clumsy maneuverability. For each additional point of essence invested in this ability the suqur’s fly speed increases by 5 feet and his maneuverability increases by one step.

• Talons: The suqur has a pair of talons that can be used as natural weapons dealing 1d4 damage plus the suqur’s Strength modifier. The suqur can only use these talons to attack if he is grappling or if he is flying at least 5 feet above the ground.

• Falcon Blood: A suqur gains a +2 racial bonus to Fly and Perception checks.

• Bonus Essence: Suqur have a natural connection to akashic energy and start play with 1 bonus essence point in their pool.

• Languages: Suqur begin play speaking Common. Suqur with high Intelligence scores can choose from the following: Auran, Elven, Gnome, Halfling and Sylvan.

Akashic Racial Traits

All akashic creatures have the following racial traits unless noted otherwise:

• Essence Pool equal to racial hit die.

• Immunity to sleep effects.

• Low-light vision.

• Unless otherwise noted, akashic creatures always use Charisma as their primary veilweaving modifier. Akashic creatures with class levels use the primary veilweaving modifier of their class.
description:
The suqur have humanoid bodies with the heads, wings, and talons of a falcon. They are a solitary and nomadic folk, difficult to befriend and fierce when offended.

Physical Description: Suqur have slight frames, typically standing no more than five and a half feet tall, though their wings typically measure ten to twelve feet across when fully extended. Not all suqur have the strength to sustain true flight with these wings, though they can use them to glide from place to place. They have the piercing eyes and curving beaks of a bird of prey, and their feet are powerful grasping talons. Their feathers span a range of colors including sandy brown, snow white, and speckled gray. Suqur rarely wear jewelry, but will often dye the tips of their wing feathers bright and exotic colors.

Society: Suqur rarely settle down for any length of time and are almost always on the move, following a deep-seated need to travel and explore. When a suqur meets a potential mate they will typically stay together long enough to reproduce and for their hatchlings to reach adolescence, usually around their eighth birthday. At this point, the family will usually separate with the offspring following whichever parent their temperaments and natural gifts are most suited for, usually spending the next two to three years learning the skills they will need to survive on their own before going their own way.

Relations: Suqur carry few preconceptions about other races, instead choosing to evaluate each creature or society they come across based on the merits or faults they personally observe. The exception to this are the gnome and halfling peoples, who the suqur adore. Whenever a suqur settles down for any length of time, it is usually near a community of such folks, with whom the suqur trade their skills as scouts and protectors for food and pleasant company.

Alignment and Religion: Suqur are independent and nomadic folk, and as such have a difficult time understanding the need of other beings to codify and regulate activities and social interactions. Suqur are almost always chaotic

Adventurers: The nomadic lifestyle of the suqur lends itself readily to adventuring, as do their natural skills as scouts. The suqur’s lack of racial preconceptions allows them to work with a wide variety of groups, though a party seeking a suqur’s services will need to be an active group as a bored suqur is likely to leave them for something more stimulating.

name: Tuktu
racial_traits:
• +2 Dexterity, +2 Strength, –2 Intelligence: While nimble footed and powerfully built, tuktu are not known for having swift minds.

• Essence-Born: Tuktu are humanoids with the akashic subtype.

• Medium: Tuktu are medium creatures and have no bonuses or penalties due to their size.

• Base Speed: Tuktu have a base speed of 40 feet.

• Bonus Essence: Tuktu have a natural connection to the unique power of akasha and start play with 1 bonus essence point in their pool.

• Gore: The tuktu’s large rack of antlers grants them a gore primary natural attack dealing 1d6 damage plus their Strength modifier. This gore can be enhanced by investing essence in it; the tuktu gains an enhancement bonus to attack and damage with their gore attack equal to the amount of essence invested.

• Herd Bond (Su): When a tuktu receives a numeric bonus from a teamwork feat, or an aid another action, that bonus is increased by +1. This ability can be invested with essence. For each point of essence invested in this ability the bonuses granted by teamwork feats and aid another actions increases by an additional +1.

• Teamwork: Tuktu begin play with a teamwork feat of their choice that they meet the prerequisites for, as a bonus feat.

• Tundra Born: A tuktu’s large, cloven hooves and dense coat makes them well adapted to cold environments. They can move through non-magical difficult terrain caused by ice and snow at normal speed and gain +2 racial bonus on Fortitude saving throws against exposure to cold weather. However, they also suffer a -1 penalty on Fortitude saves to resist warm or hot weather.

• Languages: Tuktu begin play speaking Common and Sylvan. Tuktu with high Intelligence scores can choose from the following: Elven, Giant, Gnome, and Halfling.

Akashic Racial Traits

All akashic creatures have the following racial traits unless noted otherwise:

• Essence Pool equal to racial hit die.

• Immunity to sleep effects.

• Low-light vision.

• Unless otherwise noted, akashic creatures always use Charisma as their primary veilweaving modifier. Akashic creatures with class levels use the primary veilweaving modifier of their class.

Alternate Racial Traits

• Hearty: While most tuktu are known for their cooperative nature, some are noted for being being tireless, and receive receive Endurance as as a bonus feat at 1st level. This racial trait replaces teamwork.

• Outcast: Some tuktu for various reasons live their lives alone. Forced to make their way without a tribe to work with, these solitary tuktu have become uncommonly self sufficient. Outcast tuktu gain a +1 racial bonus to Heal and Survival checks. This ability can be invested with essence. For each point of essence invested in this ability their racial bonuses to Heal and Survival increases by +1. This racial trait replaces herd bond.

• Swift: Some tuktu are exceptionally swift, often outrunning their fellows, and receive Run as as a bonus feat at 1st level. This racial trait replaces teamwork.
description:
The tuktu are a race of cervine humanoids with many of the physical characteristics common to reindeer. In spite of their tendency to roam and wander, they are well known for their reliability.

Physical Description: Tuktu are tall and powerfully built, though their most striking feature are their massive antlers. They have dense fur coats that come in mixed shades of black, brown, grey, and white.

Society: Tuktu are nomadic traders native to cold regions. They roam their native regions in small closely knit tribes, though they are known for the ease with which separate tribes interact. As a race they put a very strong emphasis on cooperation and have a tendency to heavily focus on doing what is best for the tribe sometimes to the detriment of individual interests.

Relations: Tuktu are well known for their hardiness and reliability. They are a largely peaceful race and get along well with other races. When met with hostility they will often flee or withdraw, but are extremely fierce when forced into battle.

Alignment and Religion: Tuktu tend towards good alignments, as their society favors individuals considering how their actions affect others. While not strictly lawful, tuktu almost never have chaotic alignments. They often worship deities of community, nature, and travel.

Adventurers: Endowed with a natural predisposition to roam, tuktu are prone to striking out on their own when they becomes curious about the world beyond their tribes. Tuktu have a predisposition towards classes with a connection to nature such as huay and rangers, but the restrained and contemplative ways of the guru also appeal to some members of this race.

Male Names: Aput, Kallik, Silla, Tulok, Yuka

Female Names: Aput, Kirima, Meriwa, Tapeesa, Uki
source: Akashic Tales: Gifts of Winter Pageantry, pgs. 14–16

name: Vira-Lorr
racial_traits:
Racial Traits

• Ability Scores: +2 Dexterity, +2 Wisdom, -2 Constitution: While their glimpses of the future give them astounding reflexes and insight, their bodies have difficulty bearing the strain.

• Size: Vira-Lorr are Medium creatures and thus have no bonuses or penalties due to their size.

• Astral Born: Vira-Lorr are outsiders with the native and akashic subtypes

• Speed: Vira-Lorr has a base speed of 30ft.

Senses Racial Traits

• Darkvision: Vira-Lorr have darkvision 60 ft.

• Low-Light Vision: Vira-Lorr can see twice as far as humans in conditions of dim light.

• Seer's Sense: The third eye in their forehead gives ViraLorr exceptional sensitivity to the world around them, granting them a +2 racial bonus to Perception and Spellcraft checks.

Magic Racial Traits

• Oracle's Sight: The first Vira-Lorr were said to be born from an ancient and powerful extraplanar entity from the astral plane mixing with mortals, and its descendants carry some of its power. Vira-Lorr gain the ability to shape the Eye of the Oracle veil, granting it the Fleshwarp descriptor. They are always treated as having this veil shaped, and it does not count against their veils shaped or their veil slots and cannot be bound normally. They use their highest mental ability score as their veilweaving modifier, their character level as their veilweaving level with this veil.

• Bonus Essence: Vira-Lorr have a natural connection to akashic energies and begin with 1 bonus point of essence.

Alternate Racial Traits

Terrifying Seer: Born of an entity that disdained mortal life, some Vira-Lorr carry the subtle traces of their great ancestor's pride in their third eye. They gain the ability to shape the Eyes of Terror veil, granting it the Fleshwarp descriptor. They are always treated as having this veil shaped, and it does not count against their veils shaped or their veil slots and cannot be bound normally. They use their highest mental ability score as their veilweaving modifier, their character level as their veilweaving level with this veil. This ability replaces the Oracle's Sight racial ability.

Eye of Truth: The being who mixed its blood with the ancestors of the Vira-Lorr was said to see through all falsehoods, and some of its descendants carry a fraction of this gift in the eye upon their brow. They gain the ability to shape the Eye of Revealing veil, granting it the Fleshwarp and Divination descriptors. They are always treated as having this veil shaped, and it does not count against their veils shaped or their veil slots and cannot be bound normally. They use their highest mental ability score as their veilweaving modifier, their character level as their veilweaving level with this veil. This ability replaces the Oracle's Sight racial ability.

Gaze of the Grimm: For beings beyond mortality the truths of life and death are much like any other, and the third eye of some Vira-Lorr pierce the veil between whether they wish it or not. They gain the ability to shape the Vanth's Eyes veil, removing its Evil descriptor and granting it the Fleshwarp and Divination descriptors. They are always treated as having this veil shaped, and it does not count against their veils shaped or their veil slots and cannot be bound normally. They use their highest mental ability score as their veilweaving modifier, their character level as their veilweaving level with this veil. This ability replaces the Oracle's Sight racial ability.

Abyssal Eye: Born of an entity that corrupts mortals with its mere gaze, some Vira-Lorr carry the subtle traces of their great ancestor's taint in their third eye. They gain the ability to shape the Glabrezu's Gaze veil, granting it the Fleshwarp descriptor. They are always treated as having this veil shaped, and it does not count against their veils shaped or their veil slots and cannot be bound normally. They use their highest mental ability score as their veilweaving modifier, their character level as their veilweaving level with this veil. This ability replaces the Oracle's Sight racial ability.
description:
A race descended from eldritch beings of the astral plane, viralorr are gifted with a third eye that resembles a jewel in their forehead that sees beyond the veil, often into things best left unseen. Their strange visions and gifts often lead to them being ostracized in whatever society they inhabit, causing many to live a life of solitude. Those who can rise above this distrust often find themselves serving as oracles, seers, or shamans given their gifts.

Physical Description: Vira-Lorr tend to have slight frames, typically standing no more than five and a half feet tall. The nature of their mystical third eye saps their vitality unless they have great force of will, and they are often beset by fractured visions of the future. Their skin is pale and their hair tends towards darker colors ranging from deep red to black, though rarely a child might be born with stark white or silver hair which is often seen as an omen. The eyes of a Vira-Lorr shine like jewels in myriad colors that shift with their moods, and close inspection reveals their iris and pupil to be multifaceted like a cut gem.

Society: Vira-Lorr society is a strange thing as they often arise from other races similar to Aasimar and Tieflings, and as such they rarely form societies of their own. While many often treat Vira-Lorr as strange or foreign those who prove to be true friends often find themselves blessed by the luck that only a seer can predestine, while those who make them their enemy often find fate has turned against their every whim. Vira-Lorr can often be found in places of learning and contemplation and tend to gravitate to such places.

Relations: The life of a Vira-Lorr can be very lonely. Their visions can be controlled given training and experience but even Vira-Lorr with towering wills can find themselves beset by the sight of futures they wish not to see. This can lead to them being ostracized by those around them who distrust their gifts. Dwarves in particular tend to be distrustful of Vira-Lorr given their eldritch nature, while oddly enough Elves, Orcs, and Half-Orcs often venerate them for their visions.

Adventurers: Vira-Lorr tend to live a lonely existence, often isolated from others by their gifts and as such many find themselves drawn to the nomadic existence of adventuring. Oracles, Shamans, Witches, and Mesmerists make up the majority of Vira-Lorr adventurers, though some find themselves becoming Occultists or leaning towards more martial paths.

Non-Human Vira-Lorr: Not all Vira-Lorr are descended from humans. ViraLorr can be born of any intelligent race, though human vira-lorr are the most common. Vira-Lorr of other races tend to be more frail but also more graceful than their kin, with every movement appearing to be in harmony with the world around them. While any creature that breeds with astral entities may give birth to offspring, only humanoids can give birth to Vira-Lorr.

Non-human vira-lorr have the same statistics shown below with the exception of size. Thus a halfling vira-lorr is small but possesses the same statistics and abilities as a human vira-lorr–the difference is purely cosmetic. Non-human vira-lorr do not possess any of the racial abilities of their base race. However, they are usually raised in the same cultural context as other members of their base race, and thus generally adopt the same fighting style as their peers, use the same types of weapons and armor, and study the same skills.
source: Echoes of Magic — Those Who Listen, pgs. 47–48

name: Xquenda
racial_traits:
Standard Racial Traits

• Ability Score Modifiers: Xquenda have hearty bodies and inquisitive minds, but they prefer to take their time to think before acting. They gain +2 Constitution, +2 Charisma, -2 Strength.

• Size: Xquenda are Small creatures and thus gain a +1 size bonus to their AC, a +1 size bonus on attack rolls, a –1 penalty to their Combat Maneuver Bonus and Combat Maneuver Defense, and a +4 size bonus on Stealth checks.

• Type: Xquenda are Fey with the akashic subtype.

• Base Speed: Xquenda have a base speed of 30 feet.

• Languages: Xquenda start play speaking Common and Sylvan. Xquenda with high Intelligence scores can choose any non-secret language.

Defense Racial Traits

• Akashic Immunity: As akashic creatures, Xquenda are immune to magical sleep effects. They also gain a +2 racial saving throw bonus against any spell or effect that would modify or erase their memories.

Magic Racial Traits

• External Essence: Xquenda hair represents their connection to the world. They gain a special point of essence known as "external" essence. Their external essence cannot be invested, burned, bound, or drained normally; it still counts as being part of their total essence pool for effects such as the Essence of the Immortal feat and effects that corrupt essence.

They can use the Aid Another action to invest their external essence in the receptacle of another creature, even if it already is at its maximum essence capacity. When doing so, their ethereal hair flows towards the aided creature. If they invest their external essence in a receptacle that is already at its maximum essence capacity, both the Xquenda and the aided creature take 1d6 untyped damage. This damage can't be resisted or prevented in any way. This effect lasts until the beginning of the Xquenda's next turn.

• Internal Essence: The body of the Xquenda is made of solidified essence, and they start play with 1 bonus essence point in their pool.

Other Racial Traits

• Akashic Aptitude: When a Xquenda takes a level in a favored class, they can choose to gain 1/5 additional essence point instead of a hit point or skill point.

• Fey-loved (Su): The Xquenda are unnaturally loved by other Fey. They treat the starting attitudes of creatures with the fey type as being one step higher.

Senses Racial Traits

• Low-Light Vision: As fey creatures, Xquenda can see twice as far as humans in conditions of dim light.

• Essence Sight (Su): The strange eyes of the Xquenda are very adept at perceiving the ebb and flow of essence around them. They gain Elevated Sight as a bonus feat even if they do not meet the prerequisites.
description:
The Feyworld and the Akashic Records are vastly different planes, but they have one thing in common; they are strange places to the eyes of mortals. The fey have existed since time immemorial, and their history has been stored within the plane of memory. The stories and memoirs of these ancient, magical beings carry such power that when intermingled with akashic essence, they can take on sentience and physical form, becoming fey themselves. These fey memories became the Xquenda (singular and plural), a fey tribe born of the Akashic Records.

Physical Description: Xquenda have the body proportions of gnomes, although they have very unusual physiques. Their skin is of a greyish purple hue, with lighter and darker variants. Their eyes are pools of black, with no discernable features. They lack body hair, and their head is crowned by a mesh of incorporeal tubes that resemble jellyfish tentacles. Xquenda prefer to wear big, flowing robes of earthen colors.

Society: As fey creatures, Xquenda prefer to live in small groups. They rarely become part of a faerie court, at least not for long.

Relations: While not very sociable, Xquenda are inexplicably well liked by other fey. The reasons for this are unclear, and even the most bloodthirsty fey tend to at least leave them alone. They treat all creatures as fully unique individuals. This tendency is so strong that sometimes they even fail to recognize distinctions like nationality, race, or even gender.

Alignment and Religion: Most Xquenda are chaotic neutral, and the few religious ones venerate the Eldest or nature itself.

Adventurers: There are more Xquenda adventurers than not, since they value practical experience over a routinary life. Their inherent akashic abilities tend to predispose them to classes that use that power, but any class has been represented among them.

Female Names: Nayeli, Donaj, Nadxié, Zhuyé,Beu,

Male Names: Nayaa, Bi'cu, Lùu, Zaac, Cayu

Unisex Names: Lanni, Guirá, Inda Jani, Lani

name: Yōsei
racial_traits:
• 2 Con, +2 Dex, +2 Cha, -4 Str: Yōsei are charming, agile, and almost glow with good health. However, due in part to their size, they have little in the way of physical strength.

• Essence-Evolved: Yōsei are humanoids with the akashic subtype.

• Tiny: Like other tiny creatures, yōsei take up a space of 2-1/2 feet by 2-1/2 feet, with a reach of 0. They gain a +2 size bonus to AC and attack rolls, but suffer a -2 penalty to CMB and CMD. Finally, being Tiny grants yōsei a +8 size bonus on Stealth checks.

• Base Speed: Yōsei have a base speed of 20 feet.

• Bonus Essence: Yōsei have a natural connection to the unique power of akasha and start play with 1 bonus essence point in their pool.

• Limited Telepathy (Su): Yōsei can mentally communicate with other yōsei, and any other telepathic creature within 30 feet. This ability is otherwise identical to the telepathy ability. Essence can be bound to this ability. For each point of essence bound to this ability, the yōsei can reach out and touch a willing creature as a standard action to forge a telepathic link with that creature for as long as the essence is bound. The yōsei can communicate telepathically with such a creature as if it were telepathic while within 30ft. of the creature.

• Telekinesis: Yōsei that gain levels in a veilweaving class, or that take an archetype or class template that grants the ability to shape veils, gain the ability to shape the minor veil Cufflinks of the Telekinetic in addition to their normally shaped veils. This minor veil does not count against their total veils (or minor veils) shaped or their veil slots.

• Language: Yōsei begin play speaking Common. Yōsei with high Intelligence scores can choose any of these bonus languages: Celestial, Draconic, Dwarven, Elven, Halfling, Gnome, or Sylvan.

Akashic Racial Traits

All akashic creatures have the following racial traits unless noted otherwise:

• Essence Pool equal to racial hit die.

• Immunity to sleep effects.

• Low-light vision.

• Unless otherwise noted, akashic creatures always use Charisma as their primary veilweaving modifier. Akashic creatures with class levels use the primary veilweaving modifier of their class.

Alternate Racial Traits

• Twined blood: Twins are common among the yōsei. Yōsei with this trait that gain levels in a veilweaving class, or that take an archetype or class template that grants the ability to shape veils are always considered to have the Twinned Blood veil shaped, and do not count it against their total veils shaped or their veil slots. As this veil does not count against the yōsei's veil slots or chakras, they can still shape a different veil into the same slot as this it freely. A yōsei can only target another yōsei that is their biological twin (or other multiple such as a triplet) with this ability. This racial trait replaces bonus essence and telekinesis.
description:
Yōsei are a race of tiny humanoids that are native to Chiaroscuro Island. According to their own histories their most distant ancestors were humans that settled on Chiaroscuro Island. Over time their ancestors were changed into their current state by the influence of the divine kaiju Hakkōra and Yamira, and by the proximity of the island's native entobian population.

Physical Description: Other than their size, yōsei look similar to humans, with a tendency towards slim and delicate builds. The trait that makes it clear that they are more than tiny humans are the pair of large feather-like antennae that grow from the edge of their hairlines.

Society: Yōsei society is built around living in harmony with nature and other races. They highly value artist pursuits, and finding diplomatic solutions to problems. Their society includes the entobians of Chiaroscuro Island, and is governed largely by a semi-representative triumvirate. This triumvirate consists of a pair elected yōsei kaiju chosen, one bonded to Hakkōra and the other Yamira, and a semi-divine eulite entobian, who was born from an egg laid by Queen Hakkōra long ago, known as Triumvir-Prince Raion. However, the ultimate authority on all matters is Queen Hakkōra, and to a lesser extent Lord Yamira.

Relations: Though they do not interact often with many races, other than their entobian countrymen, yōsei get along especially well with most other races. They are especially fond of elves due to their shared love of nature, and gnomes for their vibrant and diplomatic natures.

Alignment and Religion: The overwhelming majority of yōsei are good aligned, due in large part to the influences of their patron kaiju. Yōsei generally worship Hakkōra, Prince Raion, or Yamira, with the remainder commonly worshiping nature itself.

Adventurers: Yōsei are not not naturally predisposed to taking up lives of adventuring. However, their entobian friends are. Most yōsei adventures are found in the company of groups of entobians. They have a strong predisposition for nature focused classes such as huay and druid, and healing and support classes such as bard and radiant.

Names: Yōsei names are drawn from those common to entobains and humans, but names reflecting aspects of nature such as flowers, clouds, and minerals are also very common.
